## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(╬ø, E_╬ø)`** ŌĆö finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal ŌĆö not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, Ōäż^d, ŌĆ”) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallŌĆōLiebŌĆōMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
┬¦3, ŌĆ”) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `╬ø : Type*` together with `[Fintype ╬ø]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `Ōäż` or `Ōäż^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : ╬ø ŌåÆ ╬ø ŌåÆ Ōäé` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

Generic spin `S`, arbitrary bipartite-antiferromagnetic Heisenberg coupling supported on a connected bipartite graph, with the intermediate-existence hypothesis. The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= ŌŖź` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `Ōēż 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `Ōēż 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `─ż'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 #3859 `hermitianMinEigenvalue Ōēż ╬╝` from eigenvector existence, (j.4) #3860 packaged form, (j.5) #3861 consumer-friendly conditional bound at `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (needs the `╬Į_PF = hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzŌĆōWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank Ōäé Ōēż 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D Ōēź 0` version because single-ion `┬▒2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D Ōēź 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **╬╗=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|╬ø|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `╬╗ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `╬╗ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 Ōēż N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `╬╗ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `╬╗ Ōēź 1`, `D Ōēż 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M Ōł® Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M Ōł® Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t Ōłł Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ┬▒2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 Ōēż N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`, `D Ōēź 0`; `╬╗ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ╬╗`, `D Ōēż 0`. |

The Theorem 11.11 occupation-basis machinery, re-developed generically in the basis for instantiation at the spectral eigenbasis of `T`. **Theorem 11.17 PR3 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandEigenbasis.lean`): spectral-eigenbasis annihilation peel** ŌĆö the orthonormal eigenbasis `T.IsHermitian.eigenvectorBasis` transported to a `Module.Basis` of `Fin(M+1)ŌåÆŌäé` (`eigenbasisAsBasis`), its coordinate orthonormality `╬Ż_x conj(e_j(x))e_k(x)=╬┤_{jk}`, the dual CAR `{─ł_Žā(─ō_j),─łŌĆĀ_Žä(e_k)}=╬┤_{jk}╬┤_{ŽāŽä}┬Ę1`, the smeared-annihilator vacuum kill `─ł_Žā(Žå)|vacŌ¤®=0`, and the eigenbasis-annihilation peel `─ł_Žā(─ō_j)|qsŌ¤® = ╬ŻßĄó (-1)^i┬Ę╬┤_{j,(qs[i]).1}╬┤_{Žā,(qs[i]).2}┬Ę|qsŌł¢iŌ¤®` (the occupation-detection engine). **Theorem 11.17 PR4 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpanning.lean`): eq. (11.3.46) Fock spanning (hard direction)** ŌĆö the eigenvalue equation for the transported eigenbasis, the ground-state annihilation `─ł_Žā(─ō_j)╬”=0` for every nonzero-eigenvalue mode, the eigenmode number operator `n╠é_{j,Žā}=─łŌĆĀ_Žā(e_j)─ł_Žā(─ō_j)` with its creation commutation `[n╠é,─łŌĆĀ_Žä(e_k)]=╬┤_{jk}╬┤_{ŽāŽä}─łŌĆĀ_Žä(e_k)`, its diagonality in the occupation basis `n╠é_{j,Žā}occMon(g)=g(j,Žā)┬ĘoccMon(g)` (list induction), the **coefficient vanishing** (ground-state occupation-basis coefficient = 0 on any config occupying a nonzero-eigenvalue mode), and the capstone **`flatBand_groundState_mem_flatFockSpan`**: a flat-band ground state lies in the span of the flat-band-supported (zero-eigenvalue) occupation monomials (`IsFlatSupported`). **Theorem 11.17 PR5 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandFilling.lean`): filling-refined eq. (11.3.46)** ŌĆö the total fermion number operator is diagonal in the occupation basis (`N╠é occMon(g)=|occupied(g)|┬ĘoccMon(g)`, list induction via `[N╠é,─łŌĆĀ(w)]=─łŌĆĀ(w)`), so a `DŌéĆ`-electron ground state has occupation-basis coefficients supported on the `DŌéĆ`-electron configs; combined with the flat-band coefficient vanishing this gives `flatBand_groundState_atFilling_mem_flatFockSpan` (flat-band ground state Ōłł span of flat-supported occupation monomials with exactly `DŌéĆ` occupied modes). **Theorem 11.17 PR6 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandMuTransport.lean`): transport of eq. (11.3.46) to the special basis** ŌĆö the coordinate identity `ker T = span{╬╝_z}` (finrank transport via WithLp), the invariance of the `╬╝`-Slater Fock submodule `generalFlatBandFockSubmodule ╬╝` under `─łŌĆĀ_Žā(w)` for `w Ōłł span{╬╝_z}`, and the capstone `flatBand_groundState_mem_generalFlatBandFockSubmodule`: a flat-band ground state at filling lies in the `╬╝`-Slater Fock submodule of the special basis (since the flat-supported eigenbasis monomials use only flat eigenvectors, each Ōłł `ker T = span{╬╝_z}`). **Theorem 11.17 PR7 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinConfig.lean`): index-mode number-operator machinery toward eq. (11.3.47)** ŌĆö the site-dual CAR localized on the index set I (`{─ē_{z,Žā},├óŌĆĀ_{╬╝_{z'},Žä}}=╬┤_{ŽāŽä}╬┤_{zz'}╬╝_z(z)`), the index-mode number operator `n╠é^╬╝_{z,Žā}=├óŌĆĀ_{╬╝_z,Žā}─ē_{z,Žā}` with its creation commutation, its diagonality in the ╬╝-Slater states (`n╠é^╬╝_{z,Žā}|qsŌ¤®=╬╝_z(z)┬Ęcount┬Ę|qsŌ¤®`, list induction), and the **no double occupancy of index modes** `n╠é^╬╝_{z,Ōåæ}n╠é^╬╝_{z,Ōåō}╬”=0` (operator identity = `├óŌĆĀ├óŌĆĀ┬ĘgeneralCDownUp` + `─ē_Ōåō─ē_Ōåæ╬”=0`). **Theorem 11.17 PR8 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinRep.lean`): tight `I`-mode transport toward eq. (11.3.47)** ŌĆö the special basis `{╬╝_z}` extends to a full `Fin(M+1)`-basis `e╬╝` (`exists_extended_special_basis`); an `I`-mode `╬╝`-Slater state equals a mode monomial over `e╬╝` (`generalFlatBandSlaterState_eq_generalModeMonomial`, carrying them into `generalOccBasis e╬╝` for linear independence); the tight `I`-mode `╬╝`-Slater Fock submodule (`generalFlatBandIModeFockSubmodule`) is invariant under index-mode creation, and a flat-band ground state lies in it (`flatBand_groundState_mem_imode`, resolving the `zŌłēI` subtlety). PR3b `TJAllUpGround.lean` then proves `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState_eq_zero` `─ż_tJ|ŌåæŌĆ”ŌåæŌ¤®=0` at half-filling ŌĆö the maximal-spin `|ŌåæŌĆ”ŌåæŌ¤®` is a zero-energy eigenstate; PR3c-prep `TJSingletAnnihilation.lean` adds the singlet annihilator `╬ö_xy=─ē_{yŌåō}─ē_{xŌåæ}ŌłÆ─ē_{yŌåæ}─ē_{xŌåō}` (`╬ö_xy|ŌåæŌĆ”ŌåæŌ¤®=0`), towards the bond identity; PR3c `TJExchangeBondPSD.lean` proves the CAR identity `tJSingletAnnihilation_conjTranspose_mul_self` `╬ö_xyŌĆĀ╬ö_xy = n╠é_{xŌåæ}n╠é_{yŌåō}+n╠é_{xŌåō}n╠é_{yŌåæ}ŌłÆ┼£Ōü║_x┼£Ōü╗_yŌłÆ┼£Ōü╗_x┼£Ōü║_y` (xŌēĀy) + cross-site commutes `TJCrossSiteSpinCommute.lean`, the content behind the half-bond identity; PR3d `TJExchangeBondHalf.lean` proves `tJExchangeBond_eq_half_singletNormSq` `n╠é_xn╠é_y/4ŌłÆ┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y=┬Į╬ö_xyŌĆĀ╬ö_xy` (xŌēĀy) and `tJExchangeBond_posSemidef` (the bond is positive-semidefinite) ŌĆö the per-bond PSD input; PR3e `TJExchangePSD.lean` proves `tJExchange_posSemidef` `(tJExchange N G).PosSemidef` (graph sum of per-bond PSD terms via `Finset.sum_induction`) ŌĆö the operator nonnegativity behind the half-filling ground energy 0; PR3f `TJHalfFillingReduction.lean` extends to the whole sector ŌĆö `tJFillingSector_full` (Ne=N+1 Ō¤╣ fully occupied), `tJ_kinetic_sandwich_mulVec_eq_zero_of_filling`, and `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_eq_smul_tJExchange_of_filling` (`─ż_tJ v=J┬ĘtJExchange v` for hard-core N╠é=N+1 v); PR3g `TJAllUpProperties.lean` (`N╠é|ŌåæŌĆ”ŌåæŌ¤®=(N+1)|ŌåæŌĆ”ŌåæŌ¤®`, hardcore, ŌēĀ0) + `TJHalfFillingGroundEnergy.lean` `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_zero` (`groundEnergyAtFilling ─ż_tJ (N+1)=0`: all-up at 0, PSD ŌćÆ rayleighŌēź0); axiom-free); PR3h `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyLower.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge` (`N+2 Ōēż finrank` of the half-filling ground subspace: the all-up state is a highest-weight ground state, so the SU(2) lowering tower yields `N+2` LI ground states `(┼£Ōü╗)^k|ŌåæŌĆ”ŌåæŌ¤®`; mirrors #4305; axiom-free); PR3i `TJHalfFillingBondAction.lean` `tJExchangeBond_mulVec_tJConfigOf_full` (the exchange bond is a half spin-swap on the filled sector: `(┬╝ n╠é_x n╠é_y ŌłÆ ┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y)|╬”_sŌ¤®=┬Į(|╬”_sŌ¤®ŌłÆ|╬”_{tJSpinSwap s x y}Ō¤®)` via the sign-free swap over the 4 spin cases; ground states of `tJExchange` have spin-swap-invariant bond amplitudes ŌĆö toward the degeneracy upper bound; axiom-free); PR3i-2 `TJHalfFillingBondGround.lean` `tJ_ground_bond_mulVec_eq_zero` (a half-filling ground state is annihilated by every adjacent bond: `bond_xy v=0` via the PSD sum `Ō¤©v,tJExchange vŌ¤®=0` ŌćÆ each `Ō¤©v,bond_xy vŌ¤®=0` ŌćÆ `╬ö_xy v=0`; axiom-free); PR3i-3a `TJHalfFillingAmplitude.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_swap_invariant` (half-filling ground amplitudes are spin-swap invariant: `v(tJConfigOf s)=v(tJConfigOf(tJSpinSwap s x y))` for adjacent bonds, via filling-basis expansion + the bond half-swap action + `bond_xy v=0`; `tJFillingSwap` sector permutation; axiom-free); PR3i-3b `TJHalfFillingUpCount.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_eq_of_same_upCount` (half-filling ground amplitudes depend only on the up-count: equal up-counts Ō¤╣ equal value-counts Ō¤╣ adjacent-swap reachable [`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`] Ō¤╣ amplitudes equal by reachability induction over the per-bond swap invariance; axiom-free); PR3i-3c `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyUpper.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` (**upper bound `finrank G Ōēż N+2`**: ground states determined by amplitudes constant on the N+2 up-count classes Ō¤╣ injective `G Ōå¬ (Fin(N+2)ŌåÆŌäé)`; with the lower bound #4326 Ō¤╣ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne Ōēż K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged ┬¦A.3.2). Tasaki ┬¦11.5.3 metallic-ferromagnetism Theorem 11.26 complete + **`theorem_11_26` (PROVED, Issue #4314)** (d=1, `NeŌēżK+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `NŌēżL`) ŌćÆ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian ŌĆ”) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` ŌĆö ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`, so the model and Theorem 11.26 are proved modulo `lemma_11_25` (A.17 now discharged); Theorem 11.27 still a documented axiom); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`─ł_Žā(Žå)=╬Ż_x Žå(x)─ē_{x,Žā}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **┬¦11.5.4 TanakaŌĆōTasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) ŌĆö ┬¦11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `╬ø=E├Ś{1,2,3}Ōł¬I├Ś{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)ŌĆō(11.5.22), `├ó` with `1/ŌłÜ(3+4╬Į┬▓)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`╬Ż_{Ō¤©p,qŌ¤®}(ŌłÆs ├óŌĆĀ├óŌłÆt b╠éŌĆĀb╠é)+uŌéü╬Żb╠éŌĆĀb╠é+uŌéé╬Żd╠éŌĆĀd╠é`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uŌéé,UŌåæŌł×` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`d╠é╬”=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uŌéé,U=Ōł×` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uŌéü>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1ŌēżNeŌēż2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dŌēżNŌēż2L^d`) ŌćÆ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian ŌĆ”) Ne ŌŖō ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` ŌĆö taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uŌéé,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

`EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`├éŌēżB╠é ŌćÆ Tr e^├é Ōēż Tr e^B╠é`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^├é = ╬Ż e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `─ż=╬Ż─źŌ▒╝`, `─źŌ▒╝Ōēź╬ĄŌ▒╝`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate ŌćÆ ground state at `╬Ż╬ĄŌ▒╝`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`├éŌēź0 Ōł¦ Ō¤©╬”|├é|╬”Ō¤®=0 ŌćÆ ├é╬”=0`; `├é=B╠éŌĆĀB╠é ŌćÆ B╠é╬”=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `─┤┬▓╬”=0 ŌćÆ ─┤ŌüĮßĄģŌüŠ╬”=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`─ż_v=─żŌéĆ+vV╠é`, `V╠éŌēź0`: `vŌåæŌł×` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `╬”` satisfy the effective Schr├Čdinger eq `P╠éŌéĆ─żŌéĆ╬”=E╬”` on `HŌéĆ=ker V╠é`) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto: the paired eigenvalue relation gives `v┬ĘŌ¤©╬”_v|V╠é|╬”_vŌ¤® = E_vŌĆ¢╬”_vŌĆ¢┬▓ŌłÆŌ¤©╬”_v|─żŌéĆ|╬”_vŌ¤®`, the converging right side times `vŌü╗┬╣ŌåÆ0` kills the limiting quadratic form, Lemma A.11 kills `V╠é╬”`; pairing with `Žł Ōłł ker V╠é` removes the `vV╠é` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **┬¦A.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) ŌĆö operator identity `─┤Ōü╗─┤Ōü║=─┤┬▓ŌłÆ─┤┬│(─┤┬│+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`ŌłÆJŌēżM<J`, `╬”ŌēĀ0` ŌćÆ `─┤Ōü║╬”ŌēĀ0`) + raising **membership** (`─┤Ōü║╬”ŌłłH_{J,M+1}`, via `[─┤┬│,─┤Ōü║]=─┤Ōü║` + `[─┤┬▓,─┤Ōü║]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`─┤Ōü║─┤Ōü╗=─┤┬▓ŌłÆ─┤┬│(─┤┬│ŌłÆ1)` eq A.3.8, `─┤Ōü╗╬”ŌēĀ0` for `ŌłÆJ<MŌēżJ`, `─┤Ōü╗╬”ŌłłH_{J,MŌłÆ1}`) **proved** ŌĆö A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `ŌĆ¢─┤┬▒╬”ŌĆ¢┬▓={J(J+1)ŌłÆM(M┬▒1)}ŌĆ¢╬”ŌĆ¢┬▓` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ─┤^╬▒) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`ŌłÆJŌēżMŌēżJ`, i.e. `JŌłÆM,J+MŌēź0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `─┤Ōü║`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JŌłÆM Ōłł ŌäżŌēź0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(─┤┬╣,ŌłÆ─┤┬▓,ŌłÆ─┤┬│)` gives `J+M Ōłł ŌäżŌēź0`, so `2J=(JŌłÆM)+(J+M)ŌłłŌäżŌēź0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `─ż` (`[─ż,─┤ßĄģ]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,JŌłÆk}` (`kŌēż2J`) ŌĆö `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is Ōēź`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `─ż` has an eigenstate with `─┤┬│=0` or `─┤┬│=1/2` ŌĆö `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `─┤┬▓` being PSD and commuting with `─┤┬│`/`─ż`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronŌĆōFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c┬Ę1ŌłÆM)` irreducible (off-diag `Ōēż0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector ŌĆö `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzŌĆōWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D VŌĆĀ`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d Ōēź 0`) ŌĆö **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `Aß┤┤A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `╬╗_i Ōēź 0`, `d_i = ŌłÜ╬╗_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iŌü╗┬╣┬ĘA v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vß┤┤`, `C = V D Vß┤┤` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear ŌłŚ-automorphism of `M(H)` Ō¤╣ `╬ō(├é)=├øŌĆĀ├é├ø`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear Ō¤╣ `├øŌĆĀ├é╠ä├ø`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P PŌĆ▓]` Ō¤╣ (anti)unitary `V╠é`) ŌĆö documented axioms over `Matrix D D Ōäé` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachŌĆōAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-ŌłŚ-continuous `Žå : WeakDual Ōäé A` with `Žå 1 = 1`, `0 Ōēż Žå(aŌĆĀa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-ŌłŚ compact, documented axiom) ŌĆö `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `╬┤=[─ż,┬Ę]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 Ōēż Žē(aŌĆĀ┬Ę╬┤a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`╬│>0`, `Žē(aŌĆĀ╬┤a) Ōēź ╬│Žē(aŌĆĀa)` on `Žē(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state Ō¤║ `Žē(─ż_L)` least over states agreeing outside ╬ø_L, documented axiom) ŌĆö `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` ŌĆö every state `Žü` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_Žü, ŽĆ_Žü, ╬®_Žü)` (`ŌłŚ`-representation `ŽĆ : A ŌåÆŌŗåŌéÉ[Ōäé] (HŌåÆL H)` + cyclic `╬®`) with `Žü(├é)=Ō¤©╬®,ŽĆ(├é)╬®Ō¤®` and `{ŽĆ(├é)╬®}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **┬¦A.6ŌĆō┬¦A.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete ŌåÆ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1ŌĆōA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (┬¦11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** ŌĆö the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `ŌäØ`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `vŌü╗┬╣`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4ŌĆōA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7ŌĆōA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantŌĆōFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9ŌĆōA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13ŌĆōA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronŌĆōFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzŌĆōWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19ŌĆōA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** ŌĆö the
  operator-algebraic **A.21ŌĆōA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachŌĆōAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* ŌĆö they record exactly what Tasaki proves ŌĆö but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. They fall into two categories:

- **Operator-algebraic results** (Appendix A.21ŌĆōA.28): the heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic structures (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-ŌłŚ
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**.

- **Perturbation-theoretic results** (e.g., **Lemma 10.1** (Tasaki ┬¦10.1, degenerate
perturbation theory) and singular-perturbation arguments in Chapter 10): the analytic
proofs of weak-coupling continuation and adiabatic following for eigenstate families
are **not undertaken** as an active project goal; such techniques naturally belong to
a separate analytic-perturbation development. **Theorem 10.4** (Lieb's repulsive-Hubbard
half-filling ground state) currently has its entire content axiomatized: the global minimum
energy, ground-state degeneracy, and total-spin values are all undischarged. (The fixed-┼£┬│-sector
ground-state uniqueness has been proved; full theorem discharge is tracked in Issue #5004.)

- **Book theorems that Tasaki states without proof** (results he quotes from the
external literature rather than proving in the text): **Theorem 10.11** (KuboŌĆōKishi
finite-temperature charge/pairing susceptibility bound, Tasaki ┬¦10.2.5, citing
KuboŌĆōKishi, *Phys. Rev. B* **41**, 4866 (1990)) and **Theorem 11.13** (Mielke's
flat-band ferromagnetism, `mielke_theorem_11_13`) are recorded as **faithful documented
axioms** on the concrete finite-volume operators (here the Duhamel susceptibilities),
matching the "Tasaki states it without proof" policy ŌĆö the reproving of the cited
external work is not an active project goal.

Accordingly the project's policy is to **axiomatize only the appendix and
perturbation-theory results that Tasaki's formalized main development actually uses**,
to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the tools, and otherwise to
leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in large bespoke developments whose
natural home is elsewhere. The `#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes
the precise dependency on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬¦2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
┬¦2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(Žā^╬▒)┬▓ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(Žā^╬▒)ŌĆĀ = Žā^╬▒` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(Žā^╬▒)┬▓ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `Žā^x Žā^y = i┬ĘŽā^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `Žā^╬▒ Žā^╬▓ + Žā^╬▓ Žā^╬▒ = 0` (╬▒ ŌēĀ ╬▓) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### ZŌéé ├Ś ZŌéé representation (Tasaki ┬¦2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The ZŌéé ├Ś ZŌéé structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

| `spinOneUnit{01,02,10,12,20,21}` | the six off-diagonal matrix units `\|Žł^ŽäŌ¤®Ō¤©Žł^Žā\|` (Žä ŌēĀ Žā) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,12}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/ŌłÜ2) ┼£^- ┬Ę P_Žā` for the two single-step lowering units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/ŌłÜ2) ┼£^+ ┬Ę P_Žā` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit02_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼£^-)┬▓ ┬Ę P_+` for the double-step lowering unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit20_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼£^+)┬▓ ┬Ę P_-` for the double-step raising unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOne_decomposition` | every 3├Ś3 complex matrix is a linear combination of the 9 matrix units (entry-wise); combined with the polynomial expressions above this gives Tasaki Problem 2.1.a for `S = 1` | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S Ōēź 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : ŌäĢ`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ┬¦2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) Ōäé`, with `N = 2S : ŌäĢ` (so `N = 1` Ōćö `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` Ōćö `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ┬¦2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `┼£^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, ŌĆ”, -N/2)` for `N : ŌäĢ` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `ŌłÜ(k┬Ę(N ŌłÆ k + 1))` (resp. `ŌłÜ((N ŌłÆ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `┼£^(1) := (┼£^+ + ┼£^-) / 2`, `┼£^(2) := (┼£^+ ŌłÆ ┼£^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `┼£^+` annihilates `\|NŌ¤®` (highest weight); `┼£^-` annihilates `\|0Ō¤®` (lowest weight) ŌĆö the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^+] = ┼£^+` and `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^-] = -┼£^-`: `┼£^┬▒` shift the magnetic quantum number by `┬▒1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (╬▓-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(┼£^+)ß┤┤ = ┼£^-`, `(┼£^-)ß┤┤ = ┼£^+`, and Hermiticity of `┼£^{(╬▒)}` for `╬▒ Ōłł {1, 2, 3}` (╬▓-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `┼£^{(1)}` and `┼£^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(╬┤_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(┼£^{(3)} ŌłÆ ╬╗_j ŌĆó 1) ┬Ę P_k = (╬╗_k ŌłÆ ╬╗_j) ┬Ę P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (╬▓-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `ŌłÅ_{j ŌēĀ k} (┼£^{(3)} ŌłÆ ╬╗_j ŌĆó 1) / (╬╗_k ŌłÆ ╬╗_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `┼£^{(3)} ┬Ę P_k = ╬╗_k ┬Ę P_k` (where `╬╗_k = (N : Ōäé)/2 ŌłÆ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ┬Ę ┼£^{(3)} = ╬╗_k ┬Ę P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (╬▓-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ┬Ę P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ┬Ę A`). Specialised to `┼£^┬▒` produces off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6 of Issue #458) ŌĆö the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `┼£^+ ┬Ę P_{k+1} ┬Ę ┼£^- = (k + 1)(N ŌłÆ k) ┬Ę P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `┼£^+ ┬Ę P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `┼£^- ┬Ę P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `┼£^┬▒` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (╬▓-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `╬Ż_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6, ╬▓-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(Ōäé)` (╬▓-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `┼£^+ ┬Ę P_{i+1} = ŌłÜ((i+1)(NŌłÆi)) ┬Ę E_{i, i+1}` and `┼£^- ┬Ę P_i = ŌłÜ((NŌłÆi)(i+1)) ┬Ę E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (╬▓-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `┼£^┬▒` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = diag((N/2 ŌłÆ i)┬▓)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ę 1` for general spin (╬▓-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^+ ┬Ę ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ŌłÆ i))`. The product is diagonal because `┼£^+[i, l] ┬Ę ┼£^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `┼£^- ┬Ę ┼£^+` and `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓`, this assembles the Casimir identity (╬▓-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^- ┬Ę ┼£^+ = diag(i ┬Ę (N ŌłÆ i + 1))`. Symmetric to ╬▓-12 with `┼£^┬▒` swapped (╬▓-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ę 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ┬Ę 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ = (1/2)(┼£^+ ┬Ę ┼£^- + ┼£^- ┬Ę ┼£^+)` is proved using `module` (╬▓-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `┼£^{(3)} ┬Ę \|kŌ¤® = (N/2 ŌłÆ k) ┬Ę \|kŌ¤®`, where `\|kŌ¤® := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) ŌåÆ Ōäé)` (╬▓-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `┼£^+ ┬Ę \|kŌ¤® = ŌłÜ(k(N ŌłÆ k + 1)) ┬Ę \|k ŌłÆ 1Ō¤®` for `k Ōēź 1`, and `┼£^- ┬Ę \|kŌ¤® = ŌłÜ((N ŌłÆ k)(k + 1)) ┬Ę \|k + 1Ō¤®` for `k Ōēż N ŌłÆ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (╬▓-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(┼£)┬▓ ┬Ę \|kŌ¤® = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ę \|kŌ¤®`. Direct consequence of `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ę 1` (╬▓-14) ŌĆö every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (╬▓-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `┼£^{(╬▒)}` and `┼£^┬▒` commutes with the Casimir `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬Ę 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (╬▓-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[┼£^+, ┼£^-] = 2 ┬Ę ┼£^{(3)}`. Combines ╬▓-12 (`┼£^+ ┬Ę ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ŌłÆ i))`) and ╬▓-13 (`┼£^- ┬Ę ┼£^+ = diag(i (N ŌłÆ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) ŌłÆ i(N-i+1) = N ŌłÆ 2i = 2(N/2 ŌłÆ i)` matches `2 ┬Ę ┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}] = i ┬Ę ┼£^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2` and `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ŌłÆ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) ŌłÆ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ┼£^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (╬▓-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}] = i ┬Ę ┼£^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 (`[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^┬▒] = ┬▒┼£^┬▒`) via `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ŌłÆ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-┼£^+ ŌłÆ ┼£^-) = (-1/(2i)) ┬Ę 2 ┼£^{(1)} = i ┬Ę ┼£^{(1)}` (╬▓-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^{(1)}] = i ┬Ę ┼£^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 via `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (┼£^+ ŌłÆ ┼£^-) = (1/2) ┬Ę (2i) ┬Ę ┼£^{(2)} = i ┬Ę ┼£^{(2)}`. Together with ╬▓-20 and ╬▓-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (╬▓-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kŌ¤®Ō¤©k|`: `P_k ┬Ę P_k = P_k` and `P_i ┬Ę P_j = 0` for `i ŌēĀ j`. Combined with ╬▓-9 (`Ōłæ_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (╬▓-25+) ŌĆö lets us pull a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (╬▓-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (┼£^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = ŌłÅ_{j ŌēĀ k} (┼£^{(3)} ŌłÆ ╬╗_j ŌĆó 1)/(╬╗_k ŌłÆ ╬╗_j)` with `╬╗_j = (N : Ōäé)/2 ŌłÆ j.val`. Combines ╬▓-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`**, which combined with ╬▓-9 (`Ōłæ P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1╠é` (╬▓-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `┼£^+ = ┼£^{(1)} + i ┬Ę ┼£^{(2)}` and `┼£^- = ┼£^{(1)} ŌłÆ i ┬Ę ┼£^{(2)}`. The defining identities `┼£^{(1)} = (1/2)(┼£^+ + ┼£^-)`, `┼£^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(┼£^+ ŌłÆ ┼£^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (╬▓-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k Ōłł Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k Ōłł Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (╬▓-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (╬▓-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `┼£^+, ┼£^- Ōłł Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of ╬▓-26 (`┼£^┬▒ = ┼£^{(1)} ┬▒ i ┬Ę ┼£^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `Ōäé`-linear combinations (╬▓-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} Ōłł Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Combines ╬▓-10 (`┼£^+ ┬Ę P_{i+1} = ŌłÜ((i+1)(N-i)) ┬Ę E_{i, i+1}`) with ╬▓-27 (`P_k Ōłł adjoin`) and ╬▓-28 (`┼£^┬▒ Ōłł adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} Ōłł Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is ╬▓-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ┬Ę E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (╬▓-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to ╬▓-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is ╬▓-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is ╬▓-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (╬▓-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **­¤Ä» Tasaki ┬¦2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `Ōäé^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin Ōäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}} = ŌŖż`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} Ōłł adjoin` (╬▓-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = Ōłæ_{i,j} M_{i,j} ŌĆó E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (╬▓-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `Žā : ╬ø ŌåÆ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `┼£_i^{(╬▒)}, ┼£_i^┬▒`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Phase B-╬▓ ╬▓-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSiteCore.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

### Time-reversal map for `S = 1/2` (Tasaki ┬¦2.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬¦2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `╬ø` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `╬ø = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp ╬ø` | `Matrix (╬ø ŌåÆ Fin 2) (╬ø ŌåÆ Fin 2) Ōäé` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : ╬ø` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian ŌåÆ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x ŌēĀ y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ┬Ę onSite i B = onSite i (A ┬Ę B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `Žł` also fixes `Žł` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(A┬ĘB)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanŌĆōWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### HorschŌĆōvon der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki ┬¦3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3ŌĆō10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki ┬¦3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)ŌĆō(3.4.12), pp. 66ŌĆō67.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `horsch_vonderLinden_lowLying` | for Hermitian `H` with min eigenvalue `EŌéĆ = eigenvalues iŌéĆ`, a unit state `╬ō` orthogonal to the ground eigenvector with `Ō¤©╬ō,H╬ōŌ¤® Ōēż EŌéĆ+╬┤` yields an energy eigenstate `j ŌēĀ iŌéĆ` with `EŌéĆ Ōēż E_j Ōēż EŌéĆ+╬┤` (a low-lying state; possibly another ground state if degenerate, as Tasaki notes) | `Quantum/HorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `kaplan_horsch_vonderLinden_order_lower_bound` | **Theorem 3.2, finite-volume core** (┬¦3.4; the thermodynamic double limit 3.4.22 is not formalized): for the field-perturbed ground state `╬©` of `H ŌłÆ h┬ĘO` (`h>0`) and any trial `╬×`, the order parameter obeys `Ō¤©╬×,O╬×Ō¤® + (EŌéĆŌłÆŌ¤©╬×,H╬×Ō¤®)/h Ōēż Ō¤©╬©,O╬©Ō¤®` (eq. (3.4.21), the variational core; the double limit `LŌåæŌł×,hŌåō0` gives the ŌłÜqŌéĆ SSB bound) | `Quantum/KaplanHorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS` / `staggeredOrderOpS_isHermitian` | **Theorem 4.1** (┬¦4.1, DysonŌĆōLiebŌĆōSimon, toward; eq. (4.1.7)): the staggered (N├®el) order operator `├ö = ╬Ż_x ╬Ą_x ┼£_x^(3)` (`╬Ą_x = ┬▒1` sublattice sign) and its Hermiticity (real ┬▒1 combination of Hermitian `┼£_x^(3)`), so `Ō¤©╬”,├ö┬▓╬”Ō¤®` is a real order parameter. The N├®el-LRO statement needs the actual hypercubic torus + reflection positivity (false for arbitrary bipartite graphs); deferred pending that infrastructure (infinite-volume in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/DysonLiebSimon.lean` |
| `shastry_no_symmetry_breaking_1d` | **Theorem 4.2** (┬¦4.1, Shastry, DOCUMENTED AXIOM; eqs. (4.1.9)ŌĆō(4.1.10)): absence of SSB in 1D. **Tasaki ┬¦4.1 footnote 3 (p. 76) explicitly states "We do not prove Theorem 4.2 in the present book" and refers to Shastry [58] (J. Phys. A 1992) and TanakaŌĆōTakedaŌĆōIdogaki [63] (JMMM 2004); hence Thm 4.2 is a cite-only documented axiom (confirmed, not in-progress).** For the 1D AFM Heisenberg ring under a staggered field `─ż_h = ╬Ż_x ┼£_x┬Ę┼£_{x+1} ŌłÆ h┬Ę├ö_L^(3)`, the per-site staggered order parameter of any normalized ground state vanishes in the iterated limit `lim_{hŌåō0} lim_{LŌåæŌł×}` (eq. (4.1.10), ╬ĄŌĆō╬┤ form). We record it as a faithful documented axiom over the concrete ring family; the deep infinite-volume argument is faithfully axiomatized (not re-proved). The **reflection-positivity infrastructure project** (#4777) formalizes supporting finite-dim RP layers for Cor 4.3 (susceptibility no-LRO, discharged) and related Thm 4.2 RP auxiliary results, not a re-proof of Thm 4.2 itself. Defines `ringCoupling`, `ringStaggeredSublattice`, `staggeredFieldChainHamiltonianS`. **RP infra layer 1 (in progress):** `RingBondReflection.lean` ŌĆö even-ring bond reflection `ringReflect n x = 2nŌłÆ1ŌłÆx` (involutive, half-swap, staggered-sign flip); `RingReflectionTheta.lean` ŌĆö reflection map `╬Ė(A) Žā Žä = conj(A(ŽüŽā)(ŽüŽä))`, an antilinear `*`-automorphism, with the single-site bridge `╬Ė(onSiteS x A) = onSiteS (ringReflect n x) (conj A)`. **RP infra layer 2** (`RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`): the Hamiltonian ╬Ė-decomposition ŌĆö orientation reversal `J(r x)(r y)=J y x`, spin-op conjugation (S┬╣/S┬│ real, S┬▓ imaginary), `╬Ė(┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y)=┼£_{r x}┬Ę┼£_{r y}`, `╬Ė(├ö_L^(3))=ŌłÆ├ö_L^(3)` (staggered-sign flip), `╬Ė(─ż)=─ż` (reindex + adjacency + dot-comm), and `╬Ė(─ż_h)=─ż_{ŌłÆh}` (the reflection symmetry exchanging the two staggered ground states). **RP infra layer 3** (`RingReflectionPositivity.lean`): the left-half subalgebra `SupportedOnLeftS` = `B(H_left) ŌŖŚ I_right` (two conditions: entries vanish off the right-diagonal **and** are independent of the common right-half value) with closure (zero/one/add/smul, left-site `onSiteS`), `╬Ė` maps left-supported to right-supported (`theta_right`), and the reflection-positive functional predicate `ReflectionPositiveFunctionalS` (`0 Ōēż Re Žå(╬Ė(A)┬ĘA)` for left-supported `A`). **RP infra layer 4** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`): the `╬▓ = 0` base case ŌĆö `traceFunctional_reflectionPositive` proves the trace functional `X Ōå” Tr X` is reflection positive, i.e. `0 Ōēż Re Tr(╬Ė(A)┬ĘA)` for every left-supported `A`. The proof collapses `Tr(╬Ė(A)┬ĘA) = Ōłæ_{Žā,╬╝} ╬Ė(A)Žā╬╝┬ĘA╬╝Žā` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `╬╝=Žā`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A Žā Žā` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(╬Ė(A)┬ĘA) = conj S ┬Ę S = ŌĆ¢SŌĆ¢┬▓ Ōēź 0` with `S = Ōłæ_Ōäō D Ōäō`. This is the infinite-temperature trace cone on which a later layer mounts the Gibbs exponential via Trotter. **RP infra layer 5** (`RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`): the algebraic completion of the cone ŌĆö `SupportedOnLeftS.mul` (the left-half subalgebra is closed under products), `SupportedOnLeftS.mul_theta_comm` (a left-supported `A` commutes with `╬Ė(B)`, which acts on the right half), and the **weighted trace cone** `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive` (`X Ōå” Tr((╬Ė(C)┬ĘC)┬ĘX)` is reflection positive for left-supported `C`, via `Tr((╬ĖC┬ĘC)┬Ę(╬ĖA┬ĘA)) = Tr(╬Ė(C┬ĘA)┬Ę(C┬ĘA)) Ōēź 0`) plus its nonnegative-finite-combination version `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive_finsetSum` ŌĆö the cone on which the Trotter/Lie-product factors of the Gibbs exponential will be mounted. **RP infra layer 6** (`RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`): the reflection-positive trace-weight cone ŌĆö `RPTraceWeightS M` (`X Ōå” Tr(M┬ĘX)` is an RP functional), the cone-representability predicate `RPTraceConeRepS` (nonnegative finite combination `Ōłæ cßĄó ╬Ė(CßĄó)┬ĘCßĄó`) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow`, with the product closure via the **four-operator generalization** `weightGen_mul`: `(╬Ė(A)┬ĘB)┬Ę(╬Ė(A')┬ĘB') = ╬Ė(A┬ĘA')┬Ę(B┬ĘB')` (weakest hypotheses: only `B, A'` need be left-supported; `A, B'` unconstrained; specializes to `(╬ĖC┬ĘC)(╬ĖD┬ĘD) = ╬Ė(CD)┬Ę(CD)` for the diagonal cone; the off-diagonal `A ŌēĀ B` case closes the field-crossing product of `RPTwoFieldConeRepS.mul`). **Field-dependent crossing cone:** `RPTwoFieldConeRepS n N P` (field-dependent two-field cone representation: a field-independent index `╬╣` with nonnegative weights `c`, field-dependent generators `C i z` (left-supported at each field), such that `P u v = ŌłæßĄó cßĄó ŌĆó (╬Ė(C i v) ┬Ę C i u)` for every field pair; diagonal `u = v` is a genuine cone; off-diagonal `u ŌēĀ v` is the field-crossing form) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow`/`expSeriesPartialSum` (fieldwise from the field-independent cone operations) where the field-free `RPTraceConeRepS` embeds as the constant family `C i z := ─ł i` (recovered as the `u = v`, constant-in-field degenerate case ŌĆö not a duplicate). These give the shared field-crossing cone family that the three slots of the two-field reflection CauchyŌĆōSchwarz of Tasaki (4.1.51)/(4.1.69) (pp. 89ŌĆō93; DLS 1978 ┬¦2ŌĆō3) consume. Additional: `RPTraceConeRepS.rpTraceWeight` (representable Ō¤╣ RP trace weight, from the weighted cone), and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` (RP trace weights are closed under limits, by finite-dimensional trace continuity) ŌĆö the cone on which the next layer mounts the interaction Gibbs exponential `exp(t┬ĘŌłæcßĄó╬Ė(CßĄó)┬ĘCßĄó)` as a limit of cone-representable partial sums (PR #4991). **RP infra layer 7** (`RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`): `rpInteractionExp_reflectionPositive` ŌĆö for a nonnegative finite interaction `D = t┬ĘŌłæßĄó cßĄó ╬Ė(CßĄó)┬ĘCßĄó` (`t, cßĄó Ōēź 0`, `CßĄó` left-supported), the matrix exponential `exp D` is a reflection-positive trace weight. Each partial sum `Ōłæ_{k<m}(k!)Ōü╗┬╣DßĄÅ` of the exponential series is cone-representable (`smul_nonneg`/`add`/`pow`), hence an RP trace weight, and `exp D` is their limit (`expSeries_summable'.hasSum.tendsto_sum_nat`), so `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` applies. Uses the `LŌł×`-operator-norm Banach structure (`open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`), whose topology is the entrywise (Pi) topology ŌĆö the same one underlying the trace continuity, so no topology diamond. **RP infra layer 8** (`RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`): the matrix exponential and the left subalgebra / reflection map ŌĆö `SupportedOnLeftS.exp` (`X` left-supported Ō¤╣ `exp X` left-supported: the left subalgebra is closed under products/sums/scalars/entrywise-limits via `SupportedOnLeftS.of_tendsto`, and `exp X` is the limit of its partial sums) and `ringReflectionThetaS_exp` (`╬Ė(exp X) = exp(╬Ė X)`: `╬Ė` is a continuous conjugate-linear `*`-automorphism with real exponential coefficients) ŌĆö the building blocks for the `e^{-╬▓H_L}` Hamiltonian factor of the full Gibbs reflection-positivity decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondReflection.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTheta.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionPositivity.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `refLeftSum` | **RP infra layer 4b** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, ┬¦4.1, Shastry base / PR #4977): the left-diagonal coordinate `S(A) = Ōłæ_Ōäō A_{(Ōäō,Ōäō),(Ōäō,Ōäō)}`, the scalar "square root" of a left-supported operator entering the reflection-positive factorization `Tr(╬Ė(A)┬ĘB) = conj(S A)┬ĘS B`; fundamental to the genuine bilinear generalization of trace positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean` |
| `trace_theta_mul_eq_refLeftSum_mul` | **RP infra layer 4b** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, ┬¦4.1, Shastry base / PR #4977): the **bilinear reflection base identity** `Tr(╬Ė(A)┬ĘB) = conj(S A)┬ĘS B` for left-supported operators, the genuine bilinear generalization (and proof engine) of trace reflection positivity; the foundation on which the Gibbs / doubled CauchyŌĆōSchwarz layer of the DysonŌĆōLiebŌĆōSimon / Shastry argument is built (Tasaki ┬¦4.1, Theorem 4.2, as infrastructure toward its proof) | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean` |
| `twoField_product_pairing` | **RP infra layer 7b** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Theorem 4.2 / PR #4990): the two-field **SWEEP operator identity** ŌĆö the doubled DysonŌĆōLiebŌĆōSimon approximant `(g(x)┬Ę╬Ė(g(y))┬ĘŌłæßĄó wßĄó┬Ę(╬Ė(CßĄó(y))┬ĘCßĄó(x)))^m` (with left-supported kinetic family `g`, nonnegative weights `wßĄó Ōēź 0`, and **FIELD-DEPENDENT** left-supported cone generators `CßĄó(z)` ŌĆö the reflected pile carrying the `y`-field factor `CßĄó(y)`, the non-reflected pile the `x`-field factor `CßĄó(x)`) collapses into a nonnegative reflection pairing `ŌłæŌé¢ vŌé¢┬Ę(╬Ė(­ØōøŌé¢(y))┬Ę­ØōøŌé¢(x))` over the crossing family `╬║ = ╬╣ßĄÉ`, where `vŌé¢ = ŌłÅ w Ōēź 0` and the pattern-shared family `­ØōøŌé¢(z)` (left-supported, depending on the field only through `z`) carries the SAME crossing sequence in both piles ŌĆö proved by induction via `sweep_operator_identity` (the single SWEEP step) with the disjoint-support commutation and `╬Ė`'s multiplicativity. The field-free pairing is the constant specialization `CßĄó(z) := CßĄó` (Tasaki ┬¦4.1 Theorem 4.2, crossing identity (4.1.65)ŌĆō(4.1.69), p. 90). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean` |
| `twoField_product_pairing_trace` | **RP infra layer 7b** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Theorem 4.2 / PR #4990): taking the trace of the SWEEP identity `twoField_product_pairing` and applying the bilinear reflection base identity `trace_theta_mul_eq_refLeftSum_mul` termwise yields the weighted Ōäō┬▓ Gram form `Tr(U) = ŌłæŌé¢ vŌé¢ ┬Ę conj(refLeftSum ­ØōøŌé¢(y)) ┬Ę refLeftSum ­ØōøŌé¢(x)` (the doubled reflection CauchyŌĆōSchwarz pairing consumed by the next PR7b-iii-b/c layer) ŌĆö field-dependent crossing generators (Tasaki ┬¦4.1 Theorem 4.2, (4.1.65)ŌĆō(4.1.69), p. 90). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean` |
| `falkBruch_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkŌĆōBruch** (`FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the concrete ground-state FalkŌĆōBruch inequality ŌĆö for a ground state `╬”`, Hermitian `A` and a potential `y` for `A╬”` (`(HŌłÆEŌéĆ)y = A╬”`), `2(ReŌ¤©╬”,A┬▓╬”Ō¤®)┬▓ Ōēż ReŌ¤©╬”,[A,[H,A]]╬”Ō¤®┬ĘReŌ¤©y,A╬”Ō¤®`; combines the abstract FalkŌĆōBruch (`K = HŌłÆEŌéĆ` PSD) with the double-commutator = variational-energy identity | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `hermitianSubMin_posSemidef` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkŌĆōBruch** (`HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Hamiltonian shifted to its ground energy `H ŌłÆ EŌéĆ` (`EŌéĆ = hermitianMinEigenvalue`) is positive-semidefinite ŌĆö the quadratic form is real (Hermiticity, `isHermitian_dotProduct_mulVec_im_zero`) with nonnegative real part (the variational lower bound); this is the PSD `K` fed into the FalkŌĆōBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean` |
| `falkBruch_of_mulVec_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkŌĆōBruch** (`FalkBruch.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the abstract ground-state FalkŌĆōBruch inequality ŌĆö for PSD `K` and `w` in the range of `K` (`K y = w`), `(ReŌ¤©w,wŌ¤®)┬▓ Ōēż ReŌ¤©w,K wŌ¤®┬ĘReŌ¤©y,wŌ¤®`, an immediate instance of the PSD CauchyŌĆōSchwarz (`posSemidef_re_dotProduct_mulVec_sq_le`) with second argument `y`; no spectral theory / pseudo-inverse needed | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruch.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_nonneg` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkŌĆōBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state double commutator `ReŌ¤©╬”, [A,[H,A]] ╬”Ō¤® Ōēź 0` for Hermitian `A` and a minimal-eigenvalue eigenvector `╬”` ŌĆö it equals twice the variational energy of `A╬”`, nonnegative since the ground energy is the Rayleigh minimum (`hermitianMinEigenvalue_mul_dotProduct_re_le_rayleighOnVec`); the sign of the FalkŌĆōBruch numerator | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkŌĆōBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): for Hermitian `H`, `A` and a ground state `H╬” = EŌéĆ╬”` (`EŌéĆ` real), the f-sum-rule oscillator strength equals twice the variational energy of `A╬”` ŌĆö `Ō¤©╬”, [A,[H,A]] ╬”Ō¤® = 2Ō¤©A╬”, H A╬”Ō¤® ŌłÆ 2EŌéĆŌ¤©A╬”, A╬”Ō¤®` (Hermitian-shift); the denominator of the ground-state FalkŌĆōBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean` |
| `spinSDot_double_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the per-bond double commutator is the negated transverse bond energy `[┼£_a^{(3)}, [┼£_a┬Ę┼£_b, ┼£_a^{(3)}]] = ŌłÆ(┼£_a^{(1)}┼£_b^{(1)} + ┼£_a^{(2)}┼£_b^{(2)})` (the f-sum-rule integrand / oscillator strength of the staggered mode), bounded by the bond energy | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commutator_onSiteS` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Heisenberg Hamiltonian commutator distributes over bonds `[─ż_J, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = ╬Ż_x ╬Ż_y J_{xy} [┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y, ┼£_z^{(3)}]`; with the per-bond case analysis this reduces the spin-current divergence `[─ż, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` / `_right` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[┼£_a┬Ę┼£_b, ┼£_a^{(3)}] = i(┼£_a^{(1)} ┼£_b^{(2)} ŌłÆ ┼£_a^{(2)} ┼£_b^{(1)})` for `a ŌēĀ b` (only the transverse bond components fail to commute with `┼£_a^{(3)}`, via the cyclic SU(2) commutators; `_right` is the symmetric right-endpoint companion `[┼£_a┬Ę┼£_b, ┼£_b^{(3)}]`, and `_eq_zero_of_ne` is the off-bond vanishing `[┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = 0` for `z Ōłē {x,y}`); the building block of `[─ż, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` and the double commutator in the infrared / f-sum-rule bound | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `expectation_abs_le_manyBodyOperatorNormS` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`ExpectationNormBound.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the general norm-to-expectation bound ŌĆö for any `M` and a normalized state `╬”` (`star ╬” Ō¼ØßĄź ╬” = 1`), `|Ō¤©╬”, M ╬”Ō¤®.re| Ōēż ŌĆ¢MŌĆ¢` (normalized-state form of `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul`); converts operator-norm bounds into expectation bounds in the FalkŌĆōBruch argument | `Quantum/SpinS/ExpectationNormBound.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_expectation_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): in a normalized state `╬”`, the transverse bond energy expectation `|Ō¤©╬”, (┼£_a^{(1)}┼£_b^{(1)} + ┼£_a^{(2)}┼£_b^{(2)}) ╬”Ō¤®.re| Ōēż 2N┬▓` (the operator-norm bound + the normŌåÆexpectation bridge `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul` + `ŌĆ¢╬”ŌĆ¢ = 1`); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean` |

| `pair_double_commutator_eq_zero_of_ne` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorVanish.lean`, Tasaki ┬¦4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): off-pair vanishing `[┼£_x^{(3)}, [─ż, ┼£_z^{(3)}]] = 0` for `x Ōłē {zŌłÆ1, z, z+1}` ŌĆö since `[─ż, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` is supported on `z`'s two incident bonds, `┼£_x^{(3)}` commutes with it (Commute lemmas); hence the oscillator-strength double sum has only `O(L)` nonzero terms (three per `z`) | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorVanish.lean` |
