| `stagOpVec_commutator_eq` / `totalSpinSOpVec_mul_cartWord_eq` / `totalSpinSOpVec_mulVec_cartWord_singlet` / `orderComm_mulVec_cartWord_singlet` / `cartWord_swap_dotProduct_eq` | **Cartesian order-word swap-band telescoping** (Tasaki ┬¦4.2.2, Prop 4.10; **PROVED axiom-free**, Issue #4974, PR #5018): the three-part telescoping crux (push-through + singlet boundary erasure + single-swap expectation identity) that resolves Cartesian order-word contraction in Proposition 4.10's sphere-average argument. **Uniform order├Śorder commutator** (`stagOpVec_commutator_eq`): merges six off-diagonal and three diagonal commutators into `[├┤^{(╬▒)}, ├┤^{(╬▓)}] = i ╬Ż_╬│ ╬Ą_{╬▒╬▓╬│} ┼£^{(╬│)}_tot`, feeding the swap-diff factorization `cartWord_swap_diff_eq`. **Operator telescoping identity** (`totalSpinSOpVec_mul_cartWord_eq`): pushing total-spin generator `┼£^{(╬│)}_tot` through Cartesian order word `├┤^{w}` via the uniform commutator yields the length-preserving identity `┼£^{(╬│)}_tot ┬Ę ├┤^{w} = ├┤^{w} ┬Ę ┼£^{(╬│)}_tot + i ╬Ż_k ╬Ż_╬┤ ╬Ą_{╬│ w_k ╬┤} ├┤^{w[k Ōå” ╬┤]}`, where `w[k Ōå” ╬┤]` is the word with its k-th letter rotated. **Singlet corollary** (`totalSpinSOpVec_mulVec_cartWord_singlet`): on a total-spin singlet `╬”` (annihilated by `┼£┬│_tot` and `┼£┬╣_tot`, hence by `┼£┬▓_tot` via `totalSpinSOp2_mulVec_eq_zero_of_singlet`), the leading term vanishes, leaving the pure rotation sum `┼£^{(╬│)}_tot (├┤^{w} ╬”) = i ╬Ż_k ╬Ż_╬┤ ╬Ą_{╬│ w_k ╬┤} (├┤^{w[k Ōå” ╬┤]} ╬”)` ŌĆö the boundary erasure that allows Prop 4.10 to survive. **Intermediate helper** (`orderComm_mulVec_cartWord_singlet`): combines the uniform order├Śorder commutator with the singlet corollary to produce the three-index signed sum `[├┤^{(╬▒)}, ├┤^{(╬▓)}] (├┤^{suf} ╬”) = ╬Ż_╬│ ╬Ż_k ╬Ż_╬┤ (i ╬Ą_{╬▒╬▓╬│})(i ╬Ą_{╬│ suf_k ╬┤}) (├┤^{suf[k Ōå” ╬┤]} ╬”)`. **Single-swap expectation identity** (`cartWord_swap_dotProduct_eq`): the Cartesian analogue of Theorem 4.9's `orderWordProd_swap_dotProduct_eq` (Bool eigenvalue telescoping). For a singlet `╬”`, one adjacent transposition of order-word letters expands as a signed triple sum over shorter charge-removed words: `Ō¤©╬”, ├┤^{pre ╬▒ ╬▓ suf} ╬”Ō¤® ŌłÆ Ō¤©╬”, ├┤^{pre ╬▓ ╬▒ suf} ╬”Ō¤® = ╬Ż_╬│ ╬Ż_k ╬Ż_╬┤ (i ╬Ą_{╬▒╬▓╬│})(i ╬Ą_{╬│ suf_k ╬┤}) Ō¤©╬”, ├┤^{pre ++ suf[k Ōå” ╬┤]} ╬”Ō¤®`. Equalities only; real-part band, operator-norm bound, and `O(1/V)` estimate are PR-3.3 (Prop 4.10 capstone). | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTelescoping.lean` |

### BoseŌĆōEinstein condensation of hard-core bosons (Tasaki ┬¦5.1ŌĆō┬¦5.2)

| `xyHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_5_1_xy_odlro_half_filling` | **Theorem 5.1** (┬¦5.1ŌĆō┬¦5.2, AXIOM; eqs. (5.1.1)ŌĆō(5.2.5)): **off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO) of hard-core bosons at half filling**. Hard-core bosons (`uŌåæŌł×` limit of the bosonic Hubbard model, eq. 5.1.4) are **equivalent to the S=1/2 XY model** (eq. 5.1.5) via the identification `├ó_xŌĆĀŌåö(ŌłÆ1)^x ┼£_x^+`, `n╠é_xŌåö┼£_x^(3)+┬Į`, `N╠éŌåö┼£_tot^(3)+L^d/2` (eqs. 5.1.6ŌĆō5.1.7); half filling `Žü=┬Į` Ōåö the `┼£_tot^(3)=0` sector. `xyHamiltonianS d L` = the XY Hamiltonian (XXZ at ╬╗=D=0, N=1). The bosonic order operators `├ö_L^(1,2)` (eqs. 5.2.2/5.2.4) are the staggered XY-plane spin order operators. For `d Ōēź 2` (`hd : 2 Ōēż d`) and half filling, ODLRO holds with `qŌéĆ > 0` (depending only on `d`): every ground state `╬”_GS` (eigenvector + min-energy, `╬”ŌēĀ0`, `┼£_tot^(3) ╬”=0`) of `xyHamiltonianS` satisfies `Ō¤©(├ö_L^(╬▒))┬▓Ō¤®/Ō¤©╬”,╬”Ō¤®/(L^d)┬▓ Ōēź qŌéĆ` for ╬▒=1,2 (`╬▒:Fin 3, ╬▒ŌēĀ2`) and all large even `L` (eq. 5.2.5; the squared operator `├ö_L^(╬▒)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)┬▓` denominator, the intensive ODLRO density). Half filling is essential; the ground state shows LRO **without** SSB (eq. 5.2.8). Proof: reflection positivity (KennedyŌĆōLiebŌĆōShastry, KuboŌĆōKishi). The recorded statement is **uniform-in-`L` finite-dimensional** (same pattern as the discharged Thm 4.6/4.8/4.9/4.11), **not** a true infinite-volume claim; it is a documented axiom because the `d`-dim reflection-positivity / infrared-bound proof technique is intractable at project scale (the existing RP infrastructure is 1D-ring-only), not because the subject is infinite-volume. Rectification: it returns to a prove-target if a `d`-dim RP/IR-bound infrastructure is ever built | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

The order parameter obeys `m\* Ōēź ŌłÜ(2qŌéĆ)` (U(1) `ŌłÜ2`, vs SU(2) `ŌłÜ3` of Theorem 4.11). Limits in the sound eventual-`╬Ą` form (footnote 9). Conditional on ODLRO; `╬╝` parametrizes density. Proof: KomaŌĆōTasaki. **Half-filling kernel DISCHARGED (conditional)** ŌĆö the theorem `tasaki_5_3_bec_u1_ssb_half_filling` has `#print axioms` = std3 + `becMStar_ge_sqrt_twoQ` (documented `ŌłÜ2` bound) **and** additionally requires the explicit hypothesis `hRealizing` (the KomaŌĆōTasaki [66] uniform window-ratio concentration input, carried as a hypothesis with parity to the SU(2) Prop 4.10's Conjecture 4.12), tracker #5061, final PR #5069; general-`╬╝` documented AXIOM (`tasaki_5_3_bec_u1_ssb`) retained. **Half-filling discharge arc COMPLETE (tracker #5061)**: the `╬╝=0` kernel is discharged as the theorem `tasaki_5_3_bec_u1_ssb_half_filling` (predicate `IsBECCoherentSSBConstantsHalfFilling`, the half-filling kernel of `IsBECCoherentSSBConstants` adding the `┼£_tot^(3)╬”=0` sector conjunct, mirroring Theorem 5.2). **PR-1 (#5062)**: the predicate + the coherent-state Rayleigh numerator double-sum expansion `becCoherentState_dotProduct_mulVec` (`Ō¤©╬×_╬Ė,O ╬×_╬ĖŌ¤® = (2M_max+1)^{ŌłÆ1} ╬Ż_{M',M} conj(e^{ŌłÆiM'╬Ė})e^{ŌłÆiM╬Ė}Ō¤©╬ō_{M'},O ╬ō_MŌ¤®`, the algebraic core of eqs. 5.3.6ŌĆō5.3.8), std3 only. **PR-2 (#5063)**: the total-`┼£┬│` sector structure that collapses that double sum ŌĆö `towerState_totalSpin3_eigenvector` (`┼£┬│_tot ╬ō_M = M ╬ō_M` at half filling, eq. 5.3.3), `towerState_inner_eq_zero_of_ne` (distinct-`M` tower states are orthogonal: Hermitian `┼£┬│_tot`, distinct real eigenvalues), and `towerState_unitNormalize_inner_eq_zero_of_ne` (the normalized `╬ō_M` `O=1` consumption form), reusing the ┬¦4.2 sector commutators `[┼£┬│_tot, ├ö^┬▒] = ┬▒├ö^┬▒`; std3 only. **PR-3 (#5064)**: the off-diagonal matrix elements that survive the sector collapse ŌĆö `becOffDiagonal_ne_adjacent_eq_zero` (`Ō¤©╬ō_{M'}, ├ö^+ ╬ō_MŌ¤® = 0` for `M' ŌēĀ M+1`, since `├ö^+ ╬ō_M` sits in the `┼£┬│_tot = M+1` sector) and `becOffDiagonal_eq_norm_ratio` (on the raising side `M Ōēź 0`, both tower states nonzero, the single surviving adjacent element is the real norm ratio `Ō¤©╬ō_{M+1}, ├ö^+ ╬ō_MŌ¤® = ŌłÜ(D_{M+1}/D_M)`, `D_M = ŌĆ¢(├ö^+)^M ╬”ŌĆ¢┬▓ = vecNormSqRe (towerState ŌĆ” M ╬”)`, because there `├ö^+ towerState M ╬” = towerState (M+1) ╬”` exactly, so `├ö^+ ╬ō_M Ōłź ╬ō_{M+1}`), plus its lowering-side mirror `becOffDiagonal_eq_norm_ratio_neg` (for `M Ōēż ŌłÆ1` the same `├ö^+`-sandwiched element is the **inverse** ratio `Ō¤©╬ō_{M+1}, ├ö^+ ╬ō_MŌ¤® = ŌłÜ(D_M/D_{M+1})`, since there the tower is built with `├ö^ŌłÆ` and `(├ö^+)ß┤┤ = ├ö^ŌłÆ` adjoint reversal + the lowering recursion `├ö^ŌłÆ towerState (M+1) ╬” = towerState M ╬”` collapse the sandwich to `ŌĆ¢╬ō_MŌĆ¢┬▓`); std3 only. **PR-4 (#5065)**: the Ces├Āro window collapse of both complex moments (eq. 5.3.6 ┬▒) ŌĆö `becCoherent_complexMoment_raising` gives the exact finite-`L` representation `Ō¤©╬×_╬Ė, ├ö^+ ╬×_╬ĖŌ¤® = e^{i╬Ė} (2M_max+1)^{ŌłÆ1} ╬Ż_{M=ŌłÆM_max}^{M_maxŌłÆ1} Ō¤©╬ō_{M+1}, ├ö^+ ╬ō_MŌ¤®`: the `becCoherentState_dotProduct_mulVec` double sum is collapsed to the adjacent band `M'=M+1` by `becOffDiagonal_ne_adjacent_eq_zero`, the surviving phase `conj(e^{ŌłÆi(M+1)╬Ė})e^{ŌłÆiM╬Ė}` telescopes to the common factor `e^{i╬Ė}` (sign-independent), and the `M=M_max` term drops (`M_max+1` is outside the window); each summand `Ō¤©╬ō_{M+1}, ├ö^+ ╬ō_MŌ¤®` is the real nonnegative `r_M` (raising-side `ŌłÜ(D_{M+1}/D_M)` / lowering-side `ŌłÜ(D_M/D_{M+1})`). Its `├ö^ŌłÆ` mirror `becCoherent_complexMoment_lowering` gives `Ō¤©╬×_╬Ė, ├ö^ŌłÆ ╬×_╬ĖŌ¤® = e^{ŌłÆi╬Ė} (2M_max+1)^{ŌłÆ1} ╬Ż_{M=ŌłÆM_max+1}^{M_max} Ō¤©╬ō_{MŌłÆ1}, ├ö^ŌłÆ ╬ō_MŌ¤®` (collapsed by the adjoint-derived `becOffDiagonal_lowering_ne_adjacent_eq_zero`, `(├ö^ŌłÆ)ß┤┤=├ö^+`), with both-branch lowering norm ratios `becOffDiagonal_lowering_eq_norm_ratio` (`M Ōēż 0`) / `becOffDiagonal_lowering_eq_norm_ratio_pos` (`M Ōēź 1`); the window means converge to `mŌłŚ` in a later PR; std3 only. **PR-5 (#5066)**: the axis window means (eq. 5.3.7) ŌĆö `becCoherent_mean1` gives `Ō¤©╬×_╬Ė, ├ö^(1) ╬×_╬ĖŌ¤® = cos ╬Ė ┬Ę (2M_max+1)^{ŌłÆ1} ╬Ż_{M=ŌłÆM_max}^{M_maxŌłÆ1} Ō¤©╬ō_{M+1}, ├öŌü║ ╬ō_MŌ¤®` and `becCoherent_mean2` the `sin ╬Ė` counterpart for `├ö^(2)`, via the (now public) Cartesian decompositions `staggeredOrderOp1S_eq_half_smul` (`├ö^(1) = ┬Į(├öŌü║+├öŌü╗)`) / `staggeredOrderOp2S_eq_smul` (`├ö^(2) = (2i)^{ŌłÆ1}(├öŌü║ŌłÆ├öŌü╗)`), the ┬▒ complex moments (PR-4), the loweringŌåÆraising window symmetrisation `becCoherent_loweringWindow_eq_raisingWindow` (reindex `M Ōå” MŌłÆ1`, each lowering element equalling the raising element one step down via `becOffDiagonal_lowering_shift_eq`), and the Euler identities `┬Į(e^{i╬Ė}+e^{ŌłÆi╬Ė}) = cos ╬Ė` / `(2i)^{ŌłÆ1}(e^{i╬Ė}ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆi╬Ė}) = sin ╬Ė`; std3 only. **PR-6 (#5067)**: the order-parameter concentration lower bound `mŌłŚ Ōēź ŌłÜ(2qŌéĆ)` (Tasaki ┬¦5.3, "As in Theorem 4.11 ŌĆ” we can prove the bound `mŌłŚ Ōēź ŌłÜ(2qŌéĆ)`. See (4.2.39)", pp. 141ŌĆō142), recorded as a **documented axiom** `becMStar_ge_sqrt_twoQ` ŌĆö the BEC half-filling counterpart of the SU(2) concentration axiom `orderSqMoment_ratio_le_mStarSq_family` (Theorem 4.11) and the `p╠é`-mirror `mStar_eq_phat_ratio_limit`, with the `U(1)` planar factor `ŌłÜ2` (two axes `╬▒=1,2`, base ratio `ŌåÆ 2qŌéĆ`) replacing the SU(2) `ŌłÜ3`. Per the 2026-07-12 no-overreach boundary the KomaŌĆōTasaki [66] concentration mechanism is deferred with parity to those axioms, not rebuilt. `mŌłŚ` is **pinned** to its genuine value by the new predicate `IsRealizingBECCoherentFamily` (the `U(1)` planar analogue of `IsRealizingTanakaGroundStateFamily`: a **slow window** `IsSlowBECWindow d CŌéü Mwin` (monotone, diverging, eventually `Mwin L Ōēż CŌéü L^{d/2}` ŌĆö a bare `Tendsto Mwin atTop atTop` is **insufficient**: a fast window outrunning `CŌéü L^{d/2}` dilutes the normalization and admits a false witness pinning `mŌłŚ` below `ŌłÜ(2qŌéĆ)`, an over-quantification defect) + eventual `╬╝=0` half-filling GS with `┼£┬│_tot ╬”=0`, two-axis ODLRO `Ōēź qŌéĆ`, nonzero window tower states, and the **uniform window-ratio pinning** ŌĆö eventually every one-step ratio `r_M = Ō¤©╬ō_{M+1}, ├öŌü║ ╬ō_MŌ¤®/L^d` in the window is within `╬Ą` of `mŌłŚ` (math-note ┬¦3.4 refinement 2), which subsumes the `╬Ė=0` mean limit `Ō¤©╬×_0, ├ö^(1) ╬×_0Ō¤®/L^d ŌåÆ mŌłŚ` (eq. 5.3.7) **and** drives the eq. 5.3.8 second-moment concentration (`SŌéé=avg r_M┬▓`, `SŌéüŌéü=avg r_M r_{M+1} ŌåÆ mŌłŚ┬▓`) axiom-free from one source; axis-1 singlet and reversal invariance are **not** imposed, unlike the SU(2) family, so it is directly instantiable by the BEC ground state). A free `mŌłŚ` would make the bound FALSE (`mŌłŚ:=0`, `qŌéĆ>0`), hence the family pinning; the pinned bound is only the "easy half" `mŌłŚ┬▓ Ōēź 2qŌéĆ`, never the still-open equality `mŌłŚ┬▓=2qŌéĆ`. The axiom is **declared but not yet consumed** (the final discharge that uses it is a later arc PR), so all PR-6 new declarations remain std3; unsatisfiable in `d=1` (Corollary 4.3), so vacuous there. The general-`╬╝` `tasaki_5_3_bec_u1_ssb` stays a documented axiom (d-dim RP-intractability, KomaŌĆōTasaki [21]). **PR-6b (#5068)**: the exact finite-`L` band representation of the coherent second moments (eq. 5.3.8) ŌĆö `becCoherent_secondMoment1_eq`/`becCoherent_secondMoment2_eq` split `Ō¤©╬×_╬Ė,(├ö^(╬▒))┬▓╬×_╬ĖŌ¤®` into the `┬╝`/`ŌłÆ┬╝`-weighted four two-step products (`staggeredOrderOp1S_sq_eq`/`_sq_eq`), and the four band collapses `becCoherent_raisingRaising_collapse`/`_loweringLowering_collapse` (off-diagonal, phases `e^{┬▒2i╬Ė}`) / `_raisingLowering_collapse`/`_loweringRaising_collapse` (diagonal) collapse the coherent double sum to the single surviving sector band via the two-step orthogonality `becBand_ne_eq_zero_of_intEigen` and the generic `becCoherent_band_collapse` (`k=┬▒2`) / `becCoherent_diagonal_collapse` (`k=0`); entirely axiom-free (std3 only, no concentration axiom). The band-value reduction (`r_M` products, diagonal `2r_M┬▓`+Lemma 4.14 remainder) and the `(mŌłŚcos╬Ė)┬▓`/`(mŌłŚsin╬Ė)┬▓` limits are the assembly sequel. **PR-7 (#5069, final discharge)**: the diagonal `├öŌü╗├öŌü║` band collapses to `ŌĆ¢Žü_MŌĆ¢┬▓` (`MŌēź0`) and, via the exact commutator `├öŌü║├öŌü╗ŌłÆ├öŌü╗├öŌü║=2┼£┬│`, to `ŌĆ¢Žü_{M-1}ŌĆ¢┬▓ŌłÆ2M` (`MŌēżŌłÆ1`, the `ŌłÆ2M/(L^d)┬▓` residual uniformly small in the slow window `M_winŌēżCŌéüL^{d/2}`, `L^{d/2}ŌēżL^d`), and the off-diagonal `├öŌü║├öŌü║` band factors to `Žü_{M+1}Žü_M`; each concentrates on `mŌłŚ┬▓`/`e^{2i╬Ė}mŌłŚ┬▓` by the uniform window-ratio pinning (generic Ces├Āro engine `becWindowAvg_of_termwise`, `O(K)` bad set), giving `becLR_moment_limit`/`becRR_moment_limit`. The remaining two bands follow from `Ō¤©├öŌü║├öŌü╗Ō¤®=Ō¤©├öŌü╗├öŌü║Ō¤®` (`becCoherent_RL_eq_LR`, since `Ō¤©╬×,┼£┬│╬×Ō¤®=0`) and `Ō¤©├öŌü╗├öŌü╗Ō¤®=conjŌ¤©├öŌü║├öŌü║Ō¤®` (`becCoherent_LL_eq_conj_RR`); with `cos┬▓╬Ė=┬╝(e^{2i╬Ė}+2+e^{ŌłÆ2i╬Ė})` (and its `sin┬▓` companion) the four bands assemble to the eq. (5.3.8) squared-moment limits, completing all six SSB conjuncts. **`tasaki_5_3_bec_u1_ssb_half_filling` DISCHARGED (conditional); `#print axioms` = std3 + `becMStar_ge_sqrt_twoQ`, AND requires the explicit `hRealizing` hypothesis** (realizing-family existence = KomaŌĆōTasaki [66] uniform window-ratio pinning; same double structure as Prop 4.10's `#print` + Conjecture 4.12 hypothesis; requires `dŌēź2`); per-family `ŌłāmStar,ŌłāMmax` quantified outside `ŌłĆ╬Ė` (single order parameter per family); the two orphaned `├öŌü╗├öŌü╗`/`├öŌü║├öŌü╗` collapses were deleted (bands recovered by the two identities) | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensateSector.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensateCoherentMatrixElement.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensateCoherentMoment.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensateCoherentConcentration.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensateCoherentSecondMoment.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensateCoherentSecondMomentConcentration.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensateCoherentAssembly.lean` |
| `CoupledSite` / `coupledHamiltonian` / `coupledCrossCorrelation` / `tasaki_5_4_coupled_bec_ssb` | **Theorem 5.4** (┬¦5.5, AXIOM; eqs. (5.5.1)ŌĆō(5.5.6)): **symmetry breaking in coupled BEC**. The coupled two-species lattice `CoupledSite = HypercubicTorus ├Ś Bool` (`(x,false)=a`, `(x,true)=b`); total Hamiltonian `─ż_tot^╬Ą = ─ż_a + ─ż_b + ╬Ą ─ż_tunnel` (`coupledHamiltonian`, eq. 5.5.1): the two uncoupled boson copies `─ż_a+─ż_b = 2(─ż_XY,a+─ż_XY,b)` (the boson factor `─ż=2─ż_XY`, consistent with `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS`, so `╬Ą` is the textbook tunneling strength; via `sameSpeciesNNCoupling`) + the tunneling term `tunnelHamiltonian Žå = ŌłÆ╬Ż_x (e^{iŽå} ┼£^+_a ┼£^ŌłÆ_b + e^{ŌłÆiŽå} ┼£^ŌłÆ_a ┼£^+_b)` (eq. 5.5.3). Total `2N` particles = doubled half filling `┼£_tot^(3)=0`. Assuming single-system ODLRO (`hODLRO`: the uncoupled XY ground states satisfy the Theorem 5.1 bound with `qŌéĆ`, tying `qŌéĆ` to genuine ODLRO), the unique coupled ground state `╬”^╬Ą` develops a definite relative U(1) phase: Ōłā `m╠ā Ōēź ŌłÜ(2qŌéĆ)` with `lim_{╬ĄŌåō0} lim_{LŌåæŌł×} Ō¤©╬”^╬Ą, ├óŌĆĀ_{(x,a)}├ó_{(x,b)} ╬”^╬ĄŌ¤®/Ō¤©╬”^╬Ą,╬”^╬ĄŌ¤® = m╠ā┬▓e^{ŌłÆiŽå}` (eq. 5.5.5; `coupledCrossCorrelation = ┼£^+_{(x,a)}┼£^ŌłÆ_{(x,b)}`) **and** the conjugate `Ō¤©╬”^╬Ą, ├ó_{(x,a)}├óŌĆĀ_{(x,b)} ╬”^╬ĄŌ¤®/Ō¤©┬ĘŌ¤® = m╠ā┬▓e^{+iŽå}` (eq. 5.5.6; `coupledCrossCorrelationConj = ┼£^ŌłÆ_{(x,a)}┼£^+_{(x,b)}`). Double limit in eventual-`╬Ą'` form (outer `╬ĄŌåō0`, inner `LŌåæŌł×`); GS family given (unique by MLM). The two condensates are coherently coupled (entangled). Proof: KomaŌĆōTasaki [22]. Documented axiom because this is a **genuine iterated thermodynamic limit** `lim_{╬ĄŌåō0} lim_{LŌåæŌł×}`: a Tasaki footnote states that the existence of the limit itself is unproven (open in the source literature), so it falls under the open-conjecture exclusion of the externally-cited-theorem prove policy. **Completes Tasaki Chapter 5** | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

Discharge infrastructure (PR #5072): the **centered local twist generator** `M╠é_x = localTwistGen L N r x := ╬Ż_{yŌłłW_x} (2ŽĆ/L)┬Ę╬┤(x,y)┬Ę┼£_y^{(3)}` (eq. 6.2.27) over the range-`r` window `window L r x = {y : ringDist x y Ōēż r}`, with `╬┤(x,y) = signedRingDisp L x y` the **ring-distance-centered** signed displacement (`|╬┤| = ringDist`, avoiding the `2ŽĆ` seam jump of the raw angle `╬Ė_y = 2ŽĆ(y+1)/L` on wrapping windows); `M╠é_x` is Hermitian (`localTwistGen_isHermitian`), so `exp(┬▒i M╠é_x)` are unitary conjugators through the identities in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianExpUnitary.lean`. The generic norm lemma `manyBodyOperatorNormS_unitary_conj` (`ŌĆ¢U Y Uß┤┤ŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢YŌĆ¢`) is provided by `Quantum/SpinS/ManyBodyOperatorNorm.lean` for the second-order twist-conjugation bound. PR-3 (PR #5073): the **outer variational reduction** (eq. 6.2.25) is generalized to an **arbitrary** Hamiltonian `─ż : ManyBodyOpS` ŌĆö `lsm_energy_diff_symm_sum_general` (the symmetrised twist gap equals the Rayleigh quotient of `├øŌĆĀ─ż├ø + ├ø─ż├øŌĆĀ ŌłÆ 2─ż`) and `groundEnergy_le_expectationRatioRe_general` (`E_GS Ōēż` any Rayleigh quotient of a Hermitian `─ż`); the Heisenberg-chain forms `lsm_energy_diff_symm_sum` / `groundEnergy_le_expectationRatioRe` are now thin specializations, so the reduction applies verbatim to `─ż = ╬Ż_x ─ź_x`. PR-4 (CRUX A, PR #5074): the **globalŌåÆlocal twist reduction** (eq. 6.2.27) `twistConj_eq_localGen` ŌĆö for a short-ranged U(1)-invariant chain, `├ø_LSMŌĆĀ ─ź_x ├ø_LSM = exp(+i M╠é_x) ─ź_x exp(ŌłÆi M╠é_x)` (with mirror `twistConj'_eq_localGen`). Both generators are diagonal, so conjugation acts on each `(Žā',Žā)` entry by the scalar `exp(┬▒i(Žå_Žā'ŌłÆŽå_Žā))`; whenever `─ź_x[Žā',Žā]ŌēĀ0` the commutant locality (`IsLocalRangeR` ŌåÆ `isLocalRangeR_apply_eq_zero_of_far`, using distinct `┼£^(3)` eigenvalues) forces `Žā'_y=Žā_y` off the window and U(1)-invariance (`commute_totalSpinSOp3_apply_eq_zero_of_mag_ne`) forces equal total magnetization, so the global and local phase differences agree **modulo 2ŽĆ** (`twistPhase_gap`): the mean-angle term drops by U(1) and the residual `2ŽĆ┬Ę(integer)` is the periodic-seam winding `ringWrap` (spinor two-valued `exp(2ŽĆi┬Ęm)=1`). PR-5 (PR #5075, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisGeneratorNorm.lean`): the **centered generator norm bound** `localTwistGen_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` ŌĆö `ŌĆ¢M╠é_xŌĆ¢ Ōēż ŽĆ r (2r+1) N / L =: B/L` (eq. 6.2.27). By the finite-sum triangle inequality and scalar homogeneity of `manyBodyOperatorNormS`, `ŌĆ¢M╠é_xŌĆ¢ Ōēż ╬Ż_{yŌłłW_x} |(2ŽĆ/L)╬┤(x,y)|┬ĘŌĆ¢┼£_y^{(3)}ŌĆ¢`; each coefficient is `Ōēż 2ŽĆr/L` (the centering gives `|╬┤| = ringDist Ōēż r`, `natAbs_signedRingDisp_eq_ringDist`, so the raw angle's `2ŽĆ` seam spread is avoided) and each `ŌĆ¢┼£_y^{(3)}ŌĆ¢ Ōēż N/2`, over a window of `Ōēż 2r+1` sites (`window_card_le`: `╬┤(x,┬Ę)` injects `W_x` into `{ŌłÆr,ŌĆ”,r}`, injectivity from `dvd_sub_signedRingDisp`). This uniform `O(1/L)` bound feeds the `O(ŌĆ¢M╠é_xŌĆ¢┬▓)` second-order twist-conjugation bound (PR-6/PR-7), whose sum over the `L` sites gives the Lemma 6.4 `C/L`. PR-6 (CRUX B, PR #5076, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisTaylorBound.lean`): the **second-order Taylor superoperator norm bound** `symmetricDifference_conj_norm_le` ŌĆö `ŌĆ¢exp(+iM) X exp(ŌłÆiM) + exp(ŌłÆiM) X exp(+iM) ŌłÆ 2XŌĆ¢ Ōēż 8ŌĆ¢MŌĆ¢┬▓ŌĆ¢XŌĆ¢` for Hermitian `M` (eqs. 6.2.28ŌĆō6.2.30, pp. 164ŌĆō165). Setting `A = ŌłÆiM` (anti-Hermitian, `Aß┤┤ = ŌłÆA`), the matrix conjugation `f(t) = exp(tA) X exp(ŌłÆtA)` (`expConjOp`) has derivative `f'(t) = exp(tA)[A,X]exp(ŌłÆtA)` (`hasDerivAt_expConjOp`, product rule + `hasDerivAt_exp_smul_const`), so `f''(t) = exp(tA)[A,[A,X]]exp(ŌłÆtA)`; unitary conjugation preserves the L┬▓ operator norm (`manyBodyOperatorNormS_expConjOp`, consuming the PR-2 `manyBodyOperatorNormS_unitary_conj`), whence `ŌĆ¢f''(t)ŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢[A,[A,X]]ŌĆ¢ Ōēż 4ŌĆ¢MŌĆ¢┬▓ŌĆ¢XŌĆ¢`. The symmetric combination `F(t) = f(t) + f(ŌłÆt)` has `F'(0) = 0` and `ŌĆ¢F''ŌĆ¢ Ōēż 2ŌĆ¢[A,[A,X]]ŌĆ¢`, so two applications of the segment mean-value inequality `norm_image_sub_le_of_norm_deriv_le_segment'` give `ŌĆ¢F(1)ŌłÆF(0)ŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢f(1)+f(ŌłÆ1)ŌłÆ2f(0)ŌĆ¢ Ōēż 8ŌĆ¢MŌĆ¢┬▓ŌĆ¢XŌĆ¢`. The `O(ŌĆ¢MŌĆ¢┬▓)` order (not `O(ŌĆ¢MŌĆ¢)`) is essential: the first-order term cancels in the symmetric difference, turning the per-site `O(1/L┬▓)` into the summed `O(1/L)`. Replaces the book's `ad(M╠é_x)` eigen-monomial decomposition (6.2.29) by a finite-dimensional Taylor remainder (sound weaker constant; the axiom only needs `C=C(N,r,hŌéĆ)`). PR-7 (final discharge, PR #5077, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisGeneralDischarge.lean`): the **assembly** turns the former axiom into the proved theorem `tasaki_lemma_6_4_general_trial_energy_bound` (`#print axioms` = std3). `╬öŌéŖ Ōēż ╬öŌéŖ + ╬öŌéŗ` (the back-twist difference `╬öŌéŗ Ōēź 0` by the variational lower bound `groundEnergy_le_expectationRatioRe_general`), and the `┬▒╬Ė`-symmetrised sum (`lsm_energy_diff_symm_sum_general`) equals the Rayleigh quotient of `├øŌĆĀ─ż├ø + ├ø─ż├øŌĆĀ ŌłÆ 2─ż = ╬Ż_x (├øŌĆĀ─ź_x├ø + ├ø─ź_x├øŌĆĀ ŌłÆ 2─ź_x)`; each summand reduces (CRUX A `twistConj_eq_localGen` / `twistConj'_eq_localGen`) to a local-generator conjugation, bounded (CRUX B `symmetricDifference_conj_norm_le`) by `8ŌĆ¢M╠é_xŌĆ¢┬▓ŌĆ¢─ź_xŌĆ¢ Ōēż 8(B/L)┬▓hŌéĆ` (`localTwistGen_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` + `norm_le`), summing over the `L` sites to `8B┬▓hŌéĆ/L`; the Rayleigh quotient is `Ōēż ŌĆ¢┬ĘŌĆ¢` (`expectationRatioRe_le_manyBodyOperatorNormS`, operator CauchyŌĆōSchwarz), giving `╬öŌéŖ Ōēż C/L` with `C = 8B┬▓|hŌéĆ|+1`. The unused operator-form unitarity lemmas `localTwistOperator_*` were **deleted** (the per-site bound stays in the `exp(┬▒i M╠é_x)` form) | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisGeneral.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisProof.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisGlobalLocalReduction.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisGeneratorNorm.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisTaylorBound.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisGeneralDischarge.lean`, `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianExpUnitary.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/ManyBodyOperatorNorm.lean` |
| `IsHiddenAFMConfig` / `hhafProjection` / `tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` | **┬¦6.3 Proposition 6.5** (G├│mez-Santos; **PROVED axiom-free**; eqs. (6.3.7)ŌĆō(6.3.9)): the **S=1 chain on the hidden-AFM subspace `H_HAF`**. **`tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` is now a proved theorem** (formerly a documented axiom; `#print axioms` ŌåÆ `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`): the final assembly `tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` bundles, for even `L>0`, a ground state `╬” = hhafSubspaceEmbedding` of the balanced PerronŌĆōFrobenius eigenvector with (i) projection-fixedness + nonzero + eigenvector at `E = hhafMinEnergy`, (ii) minimality over `hhafRealSpectrum`, (iii) **uniqueness** (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_ground_finrank_le_one`: the restricted ground eigenspace is one-dimensional ŌĆö at `E < ŌłÆ2` the single-`┬▒` block is vacated since its eigenvalues are `Ōēź ŌłÆ2`, and the balanced block contributes the 1-D PF ground state via the injective restriction `R` into the balanced block eigenspace), lifted to the operator via `hhafSubspaceEmbedding_of_projFixed` + `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_of_projFixed_eig` + `finrank_le_one_iff`, (iv) the positive gap (`exists_hhaf_positive_gap`), and (v) exponential decay (`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`). For the spin-1 chain (`N=2`, configs `Fin L ŌåÆ Fin 3`, `Žā_x=0/1/2 Ōå” +1/0/ŌłÆ1`), a config has **complete hidden AFM order** (`IsHiddenAFMConfig`, via `IsPM`/`InCyclicOpen`/`IsNextPM`) when the nonzero (`┬▒`) spins strictly alternate around the ring with arbitrary `0`-spins between (eq. 6.3.9). `H_HAF` = span of those basis states; `hhafProjection` (diagonal projection), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS = P_HAF┬Ę─ż┬ĘP_HAF`, `hhafRealSpectrum` (eigenvalues with `H_HAF` eigenvectors). For even `L>0`, the restricted chain has a **unique ground state**, a **finite gap** (`gap>0` in the `H_HAF`-restricted spectrum), and **exponentially decaying** correlation `\|chainCorrelation\| Ōēż C e^{ŌłÆd(x,y)/╬Š}` (`ringDist`) ŌĆö so the Haldane conjecture for `S=1` holds rigorously within this artificial subspace. Proof: path-integral / 2D classical stat-mech. **Spectral foundations now proved** (infrastructure for the now-proved theorem, Issue #4718): `hhafProjection_isHermitian` (P_HAF Hermitian), `hhafProjection_mul_self` (P_HAF idempotent), `afmHeisenbergChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (defined in `HaldaneConjecture.lean`), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (compressed `P_HAF┬Ę─ż┬ĘP_HAF` Hermitian). **Ground-energy existence now proved**: `hhafConfig` (hidden-AFM config subtype), `hhafRestrictedMatrix` (H_HAF├ŚH_HAF submatrix, Hermitian), `hhafSubspaceEmbedding` (zero-extension) with the compressionŌĆōembedding intertwining `P_HAF┬Ę─ż┬ĘP_HAF Ōłś emb = emb Ōłś restMat`, and `hhafMinEnergy`/`hhafMinEnergy_mem_realSpectrum` (the minimal restricted eigenvalue is a genuine `hhafRealSpectrum` member with a nonzero H_HAF ground state), and `exists_hhaf_min_real_eigenvalue` (it is `Ōēż` every restricted eigenvalue, so a genuine restricted **ground energy** with minimality). **Per-L exp-decay** proved (`exp_decay_envelope_of_finite`/`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`): on the finite ring any state's correlation admits `|Ō¤©┼£_x┬Ę┼£_yŌ¤®| Ōēż C e^{ŌłÆd/╬Š}` (╬Š=1, C=╬Ż|corr|e^{d}) ŌĆö the decay clause of Prop 6.5 (now all clauses proved). **Max-energy infra** added: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eigenvalue_mem_realSpectrum` (every restricted-matrix eigenvalue embeds into `hhafRealSpectrum`), `hhafMaxEnergy` + `hhafMaxEnergy_mem_realSpectrum`; **spectral completeness/finiteness** (`hhafRealSpectrum_restrict` reverse bridge, `hhafRealSpectrum_subset_eigenvalue_range` via mathlib `spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`, `hhafRealSpectrum_finite`) ŌĆö the restricted spectrum IS the finite restricted-matrix eigenvalue set. **Diagonal bounds**: new general `HermitianVariationalUpperBound` (max-Rayleigh) + `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag`/`diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` give `hhafMinEnergy Ōēż (restMat Žā Žā).re Ōēż hhafMaxEnergy` via Rayleigh at the basis vector (toward non-scalarity for the gap). **Non-scalarity PROVED**: `hhafMinEnergy_lt_hhafMaxEnergy` (even LŌēź2) via all-zero config (diag=0) and domain-wall config Žā=[0,2,1,ŌĆ”,1] (HAF, diag<0); general formula `hhaf_diag_eq_succ_sum` (diag = ╬Ż_x (1-Žāx)(1-Žā_{x+1})). **Positive gap PROVED**: `exists_hhaf_positive_gap` (even L>0) ŌĆö first-excited eigenvalue EŌéü = (eigenvalues image filtered above min).min with EŌéü Ōłł spectrum, EŌéü > minEig (gap>0), and minimal among spectrum elements above minEig (via completeness). These foundations feed the now-discharged uniqueness clause. **PF real-form foundation** started: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_im_zero` (entries real), `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal` (real symmetric form) + `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal_ofReal`/`_isSymm` ŌĆö the entry point for the Perron-Frobenius uniqueness route (Marshall gauge (now built: `ringSublattice`, `hhafDressedMatrix`, `hhafDressedMatrix_eq` = ╬Ą(Žā)╬Ą(Žä)┬ĘM_real via the dressed-Heisenberg machinery) + **symmetrized-coupling infra** for the off-diagonal sign step (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_coupling_swap`: the Heisenberg Hamiltonian is invariant under transposing the coupling, via `spinSDot_comm`; `ringCouplingSym` = `ringCoupling + ringCouplingßĄĆ` with `_symm`/`_im_zero`/`_re_nonneg`; `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCouplingSym`: `H(ringCouplingSym) = 2┬ĘH(ringCoupling)`, so the directed ring Hamiltonian equals ┬Į the symmetric-coupling one ŌĆö letting the symmetric-coupling off-diagonal nonpositivity lemma apply) ŌĆö **off-diagonal nonpositivity now PROVED**: `ringCouplingSym_bipartite` (even ring `L` is bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice`: for even `L`, `(a+1)%L` flips parity, so equal-parity sites are never adjacent ŌĆö via `Nat.mod_mod_of_dvd`), `dressedHeisenbergS_ringCoupling_re_eq_half` (dressed directed = ┬Į dressed symmetric), and `hhafDressedMatrix_offdiag_nonpos` (even `L`: `hhafDressedMatrix Žā Žä Ōēż 0` for `Žā ŌēĀ Žä`, via the symmetric-coupling nonpositivity lemma) ŌĆö the diagonal `┬▒1` Marshall gauge makes the AFM off-diagonals sign-definite. **Symmetry + nonneg shift now PROVED**: `hhafDressedMatrix_isSymm` (the dressed matrix is symmetric), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_eq` (diagonal = real restricted diagonal, since the Marshall sign squares to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` (diagonal Ōēż max energy), and `hhafShifted_entry_nonneg` (for `c Ōēź hhafMaxEnergy` and even `L`, the shifted matrix `c┬ĘI ŌłÆ M` has nonnegative entries ŌĆö the nonnegativity hypothesis of `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric`). **Irreducibility started** (Issue #4732, G├│mez-Santos kink ergodicity): `hhafShifted_pos_of_ladderStep` ŌĆö two hidden-AFM configs differing by one adjacent raise/lower (ladder) move on a bipartite bond have a strictly positive shifted entry `(c┬ĘI ŌłÆ M) Žä Žā > 0` (reusing `dressedHeisenbergS_apply_re_neg_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` on `ringCouplingSym` + the ┬Į scaling), the single-edge positivity for the PerronŌĆōFrobenius reachability argument. **Ring bond graph** added: `hhafRingGraph` (cyclic nearest-neighbour graph on `Fin L`), with `hhafRingGraph_adj_sublattice_ne` (bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice` for even `L`) and `ringCouplingSym_re_pos_of_ringGraph_adj` (the symmetrized coupling is strictly positive on every edge) ŌĆö the bond-graph data feeding the per-step positivity. **HAF reachability + walkŌåÆpower** added: `RaiseLowerStepSHhaf` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf` (single HAF-preserving ladder move / its reflexive-transitive closure), `hhafShifted_pos_of_stepHhaf` (each step gives a strictly positive shifted entry), and `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable` (HAF-reachability ŌćÆ some power `(c┬ĘI ŌłÆ M)^k` is strictly positive ŌĆö the walkŌåÆpower bridge, mirroring the magnetization-sector version). **Kink-reduction setup** added: `hhafCanonical` (the all-`0`-spin configuration, base case), `pmCount` (number of `┬▒` spins, the induction measure), `pmCount_eq_zero_iff` (no `┬▒` spins Ō¤║ canonical). **Slide move** added: `slidePM` (move the spin at `a` onto a cyclic neighbour `b`, leaving a `0` at `a`), with `slidePM_apply` (its pointwise values) and `slidePM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single raise/lower ladder step on the ring-graph bond `{a, b}` when `a` carries a `┬▒` spin and `b` carries a `0`). **Slide preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity core): `slidePM_isHiddenAFM` ŌĆö sliding a `┬▒` spin onto its `0`-carrying cyclic successor keeps the strict `+,ŌłÆ,ŌĆ”` alternation, proved by transferring each `IsNextPM` pair of the moved configuration back to one of `Žā` (relabelling `b Ōå” a`) via the open-arc decomposition lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_left_imp` / `inCyclicOpen_succ_right_imp` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ` (each an `omega` calculation after splitting the cyclic wrap). **Slide lifted to a subtype step** (`slidePM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf`) and the **annihilation move** added: `annihPM` (set adjacent `a, b` both to `0`), `annihPM_apply`, and `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single ladder step when `a, b` carry opposite `┬▒` spins). **Annihilation preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN**: `annihPM_isHiddenAFM` ŌĆö removing an adjacent opposite-sign `+,ŌłÆ` pair keeps the alternation (each `IsNextPM` pair of the annihilated config either avoids the removed pair, giving an `IsNextPM` pair of `Žā`, or straddles it, where the chain `x, a, b, y` forces opposite endpoint signs), via the sub-arc lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_iff_mem` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_left` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_pred_left`. **Move-count behaviour** added: `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf` (annihilation as a subtype step), `slidePM_pmCount_card` (sliding preserves the `┬▒` count), `annihPM_pmCount_card_lt` (annihilation strictly decreases it). **CONNECTIVITY PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity theorem): the reduction measure `hhafS = ╬Ż_{┬▒ spins}(L ŌłÆ x)` strictly decreases under a slide (`slidePM_hhafS_lt`, non-wrapping successor) and an annihilation (`annihPM_hhafS_lt`); `hhaf_single_step` produces, for any balanced (even-`pmCount`) non-canonical config, a single HAF ladder move to another balanced config of smaller measure (first `┬▒` spin at `p Ōēż LŌłÆ2`, sliding past a `0` or annihilating an adjacent opposite `┬▒`); `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical` / `hhaf_reachable_canonical` (strong induction on `hhafS`) then show **every balanced hidden-AFM configuration is HAF-reachable from the canonical all-`0` configuration** (with `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf_symm` for the reverse). **Design note**: the *full* HAF matrix is reducible ŌĆö a single-`┬▒` config (`pmCount = 1`, magnetization `┬▒1`) is vacuously hidden-AFM but unreachable from `canonical` (the moves conserve magnetization), so irreducibility holds only within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector; this is the sector containing the antiferromagnetic ground state. **Balanced-sector block irreducibility PROVEN**: `hhafConfig0` (the even-`pmCount` subtype) with `hhafShiftedMatrix0` (the `c┬ĘI ŌłÆ M` block), `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical0` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf0_symm` (balanced-sector connectivity lifted to the subtype), `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable0` (subtype walkŌåÆpower), and `hhafShiftedMatrix0_isIrreducible` ŌĆö for even `L` and shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`, the balanced-sector shifted matrix is PerronŌĆōFrobenius irreducible (`isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos`: nonnegative + entrywise-positive power, with the diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` (submatrix of a symmetric matrix), `hhafShiftedMatrix0_eq` (`hhafShiftedMatrix0 = c┬ĘI ŌłÆ MŌéĆ`, the identity restricting since `Subtype.val` is injective), and `hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_finrank_le_one` ŌĆö applying `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` to the balanced block yields a strictly positive lowest eigenvector with a **one-dimensional** ground eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `┬▒1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`MŌéĆ = ╬ś┬ĘM_realŌéĆ┬Ę╬ś`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` ŌĆö transferring the **full PerronŌĆōFrobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `╬śv`, ground minimality, and `finrank Ōēż 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `Ōäé` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Remaining for the *global* ground-state uniqueness: the inter-sector energy ordering (balanced sector strictly lowest, a MarshallŌĆōLiebŌĆōMattis statement within `H_HAF`). **N├®el witness** added (toward that ordering): `hhafNeel` (the maximally-AFM configuration `+1` on even sites, `ŌłÆ1` on odd), `hhafNeel_isHiddenAFM` (it is hidden-AFM on an even ring ŌĆö all sites are `┬▒`, every `IsNextPM` pair is cyclically adjacent hence opposite parity, via `inCyclicOpen_succ_mem_of_ne`), `hhafNeel_pmCount` (`┬▒`-count `= L`), and `hhafNeelConfig0` (the N├®el configuration as a balanced charge-`0` configuration). **Ground energy Ōēż ŌłÆL PROVEN**: `hhafNeel_diag` (the N├®el diagonal energy is `ŌłÆL`, every nearest-neighbour bond antiferromagnetic `(+1)(ŌłÆ1) = ŌłÆ1`) and `hhafMinEnergy_le_neg_L` (the global restricted ground energy `hhafMinEnergy Ōēż ŌłÆL`, via the Rayleigh quotient `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag` at the N├®el state) ŌĆö the first half of the inter-sector ordering (the balanced sector reaches energy `ŌłÆL`; the remaining half bounds the magnetization-`┬▒1` single-`┬▒` sectors above this). Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` ŌĆö a single-`┬▒` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is `┬▒`), the Gershgorin-disc centre for the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors. `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_raising_lowering` / `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_lowering_raising` ŌĆö the spin-`1` ladder off-diagonal amplitude `|Ō¤©Žā'|┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y|ŽāŌ¤®| Ōēż 1` (each `ŌłÜ` factor `Ōēż ŌłÜ2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` ŌĆö for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M Žā Žä| Ōēż (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `┬▒1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym ┬Ę spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `Ōēż 1`). **Magnetization-`┬▒1` sector min `Ōēź ŌłÆ2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`┬▒` `Sz=┬▒1` (`pmCount = 1`) ŌĆö there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector LiebŌĆōMattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `┬▒` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `Žä` to be the slide `slidePM` of `Žā`'s `┬▒` spin to its new site, sharing the `┬▒` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `Ōēż ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `┬▒`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`Ōłæ_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` Ō¤╣ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `Ōēż 2`: every single-`┬▒` eigenvalue `Ōēź ŌłÆ2`) ŌĆö the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = ┬▒1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`Ōēż ŌłÆL Ōēż ŌłÆ2`). **Balanced ground energy `< ŌłÆL` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the PerronŌĆōFrobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the N├®el row gives `╬╝┬Ęv(N├®el) = ŌłÆL┬Ęv(N├®el) + ╬Ż_{ŽäŌēĀN├®el} MŌéĆ(N├®el,Žä) v(Žä)`; every off-diagonal term is `Ōēż 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`┬▒` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`MŌéĆ(N├®el,N├®el) = ŌłÆL`) we get `╬╝┬Ęv(N├®el) < ŌłÆL┬Ęv(N├®el)`, hence `╬╝ < ŌłÆL`. Since `ŌłÆL Ōēż ŌłÆ2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** ŌĆö the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` ŌĆö an odd count `Ōēź 3` is impossible. Proof: the `┬▒` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` ŌĆö no `┬▒` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `┬▒` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `┬▒` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - ┬Ę` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `┬▒` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` ŌĆö the cyclic wrap arc holds no `┬▒` spin), which would force the first and last `┬▒` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count ŌĆö contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount Ō¤╣ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`┬▒` Ō¤╣ `magSumS = L ┬▒ 1 ŌēĀ L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`┬▒` `= Sz ┬▒1`, with no higher sectors ŌĆö the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`┬▒` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish ŌĆö magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{Žā // P Žā}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`┬▒` config couples only to single-`┬▒`, an even config only to even) ŌĆö the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`┬▒` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank Ōēż 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `Ōäé` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` ŌĆö the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (realŌåÆcomplex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced PerronŌĆōFrobenius `finrank Ōēż 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` ŌĆö the **complex single-`┬▒` block has all eigenvalues `Ōēź ŌłÆ2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `Ōēź ŌłÆ2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < ŌłÆ2` the single-`┬▒` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) ŌĆö the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced PerronŌĆōFrobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The PerronŌĆōFrobenius uniqueness development ŌĆö kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`┬▒1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy ŌĆö lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

The shared `manyBodyOperatorNormS` algebra and unitary-conjugation API is hosted by `Quantum/SpinS/ManyBodyOperatorNorm.lean`; the generic adjoint and two-sided unitarity identities for Hermitian-generated exponentials are hosted by `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianExpUnitary.lean`.

### The AKLT model (Tasaki ┬¦7.1)

(2020), Lemma 7.4, eqs. (7.1.19)ŌĆō(7.1.21), pp. 186ŌĆō187): for `1 < L`, a state `╬”` is annihilated by the periodic bond projection, `P╠éŌéé[┼£_x+┼£_{x+1}] ╬” = 0`, **iff** it has the valence-bond-solid singlet-tensor form on that bond (`IsVBSGroundForm`). The concrete predicate is independent of `P╠éŌéé`: every fixed-rest-configuration two-site slice `bondSlice x ╬” Žä` must lie in the span `W = vbsBondSubspace` of the four vectors `vbsBondVec Žā Žā'`. Five independent fixed vectors in the range of `P╠éŌéé^{loc}` prove `rank P╠éŌéé^{loc} Ōēź 5`; the intermediate inclusion `W ŌŖå ker P╠éŌéé^{loc}`, together with `finrank W = 4`, ambient dimension `9`, and rankŌĆōnullity, proves `rank P╠éŌéé^{loc} Ōēż 5`. Thus `rank P╠éŌéé^{loc} = 5`, rankŌĆōnullity gives `finrank (ker P╠éŌéé^{loc}) = 4`, and the equal dimensions upgrade the intermediate inclusion to `ker P╠éŌéé^{loc} = W`. The pointwise identity `bondSlice_bondSpin2ProjectionS_mulVec` identifies the slice of the global bond action with the local `9├Ś9` action for every `x` and `Žä`. Consequently, `bondSpin2ProjectionS_mulVec_eq_zero_iff_bondSlice_mem_ker` characterizes global annihilation by membership of every slice in the local kernel, uniformly for the periodic wrap bond and for both ordered bonds when `L = 2`. Rewriting that local kernel as `W` gives `tasaki_lemma_7_4` with the same public statement as the former axiom. The separate affine identity `aklt_bond_term_eq_bondSpin2Projection` (**PROVED**, eq. (7.1.5), p. 181) relates the AKLT local term to `P╠éŌéé`, but the theorem itself uses only the local kernel equality and the global slice equivalence. Proof: explicit finite-dimensional linear algebra and tensor-slice reduction. | `mpsDualTransferMap` / `HasFaithfulDualEigenmatrix` / `mps_spans_eventually_iff_spans_for_all_large` / `mps_spans_for_all_large_iff_has_primitive_transfer_spectrum` / `mps_theorem_7_5` / `GeneratesSameMPS` / `mps_theorem_7_6` | **┬¦7.2.2 Matrix product representation.** Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, 1st ed. (Springer, 2020), ┬¦7.2.2, Theorem 7.5, eqs. (7.2.36), (7.2.41)ŌĆō(7.2.42), pp. 202ŌĆō203, and Theorem 7.6, eqs. (7.2.43)ŌĆō(7.2.44), p. 203; M. Fannes, B. Nachtergaele, and R. F. Werner, ŌĆ£Finitely correlated pure states,ŌĆØ *Journal of Functional Analysis* **120** (1994), 511ŌĆō534. The AKLT VBS state is an injective matrix product state. `MPSMatrices D N = Fin(N+1)ŌåÆD├ŚD` matrices `A^Žā`; `mpsTransferMatrix A` is the concrete D┬▓├ŚD┬▓ transfer matrix; `orderedProd` forms fixed-length words; `mpsProductsSpanAt` says that those words span all D├ŚD matrices; `IsMPSNormalized A ╬╗` is `╬╗>0` and `╬Ż_Žā A^Žā(A^Žā)ŌĆĀ=╬╗I`; and `HasPrimitiveTransferSpectrum A ╬╗` says that `╬╗` has a one-dimensional eigenspace and every other spectral value has norm `<╬╗`. The book-facing Theorem 7.5 DAG has five nodes: `mpsDualTransferMap`, the corrected faithful condition `HasFaithfulDualEigenmatrix A ╬╗ := ŌłāŽü, Žü.PosDef Ōł¦ mpsDualTransferMap A Žü = ╬╗ ŌĆó Žü`, the (i)Ōåö(ii) span-propagation theorem, the faithful (ii)Ōåö(iii) transfer theorem, and `mps_theorem_7_5` (**PROVED axiom-free; Standard 3; PR pending**). The proof handles general positive `╬╗` by `B^Žā=╬╗Ōü╗┬╣ßÉ¤┬▓A^Žā`: words acquire a nonzero scalar, while the transfer matrix and its spectrum scale by `╬╗Ōü╗┬╣`. Tasaki's printed theorem omits the faithful-dual condition and supplies no proof. This omission is necessary: for `AŌü░=diag(1,a)`, `A┬╣=bEŌééŌéü`, `0<a<1`, and `a┬▓+b┬▓=1`, the transfer spectrum is primitive but every fixed-length word span has dimension at most two; the only dual fixed matrices are multiples of rank-one `EŌéüŌéü`. The corrected hypothesis excludes this example. The concrete predicate `GeneratesSameMPS A B` requires `trace (orderedProd A (List.ofFn ss)) = trace (orderedProd B (List.ofFn ss))` for every length `L` and every `ss : Fin L ŌåÆ Fin (N+1)`. Under injectivity, `mps_theorem_7_6` is **PROVED axiom-free; Standard 3; PR pending**: there is a unitary `U` such that `B^Žā = UŌĆĀ A^Žā U`, and every unitary `V` with the same gauge relation satisfies `V = z ŌĆó U` for some `z` with `ŌĆ¢zŌĆ¢=1`. The verified DAG is `GeneratesSameMPS` ŌåÆ `exists_word_transport_algEquiv` ŌåÆ `exists_unitary_gauge_data` ŌåÆ `mps_theorem_7_6`: fixed-length word transport gives a matrix-algebra equivalence, normalization makes its inner implementer unitary, and the full matrix-algebra center gives uniqueness up to phase. | `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem75Defs.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem75Linear.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem75Choi.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem75Peripheral.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem75.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem76Defs.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem76Algebra.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/MPSTheorem76Unitary.lean`; `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTMatrixProduct.lean` |
| `bondMaxSpinProjectionS` / `regularGraphAKLTHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_7_7` | **┬¦7.3.2 AKLT model on a general graph** (Theorem 7.7; eqs. (7.3.6)ŌĆō(7.3.9)): the graph-centric generalized AKLT model and hexagonal-lattice correlation decay. `bondCasimirS x y N = (┼£_x+┼£_y)┬▓ = 2S(S+1)┬Ę1 + 2┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y`; `bondMaxSpinProjectionS x y N` = the **concrete** Lagrange/Casimir projector `ŌłÅ_{j=0}^{NŌłÆ1}(─łŌłÆj(j+1))/(N(N+1)ŌłÆj(j+1))` onto the maximal bond total spin `J=N`; `regularGraphAKLTHamiltonianS G N = ┬Į ╬Ż_{x,y} [G.Adj x y] P╠é_N[┼£_x+┼£_y]` = the **regular (uniform-spin) specialization** of eq. (7.3.7) (single global `S=N/2`, each bond ŌåÆ `J=N`; equals eq. 7.3.7 exactly on an `N`-regular graph ŌĆö site-dependent spins `S╠ä_x=deg(x)/2` are not expressible in `ManyBodyOpS ╬ø N`). On the 3-regular hexagonal lattice (`N=3`) it is `╬Ż_bonds P╠é_3` (eq. 7.3.8), the setting of Theorem 7.7. `tasaki_theorem_7_7` (**AXIOM**): for the hexagonal lattice (`IsHexagonalLatticeAKLT` marker) with VBS GS `╬”` (`IsGeneralGraphVBSGroundState` marker), Ōłā `C,╬Š>0` size-independent with sign-alternating exponential decay `0 Ōēż (ŌłÆ1)^{D(x,y)}Ō¤©┼£_x┬Ę┼£_yŌ¤® Ōēż C e^{ŌłÆD/╬Š}` (`D = G.dist`, eq. 7.3.9) and the translation-invariant infinite-volume GS is unique (`HasUniqueInfiniteVolumeVBSGroundState`). (8.3.1)ŌĆō(8.3.4)): the Haldane phase as an SPT phase. `guWenHamiltonianS L D B = anisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D + B ╬Ż_x ┼£_x^{(1)}` (eq. 8.3.4, S=1 chain + magnetic field B in 1-dir, breaks ZŌéé├ŚZŌééŌåÆZŌéé; B=0 ŌćÆ anisotropic 8.1.1). `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.3.3): the spin-`S` VBS string order `O_string^{(╬▒)}` is `>0` for odd `S`, `=0` for even `S` (even/odd-`S` distinction); `tasaki_vbs_edge_degeneracy` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` AKLT open chain has `(S+1)┬▓`-fold edge degeneracy. **SPT definition** (`def : Prop`, NOT axiom): `HamiltonianPath` (continuous `ŌäØŌåÆ` Hamiltonian, each short-range gapped-unique via `IsShortRangeGappedUniqueGS` marker); `ContinuouslyConnected`/`SymmetryConnected sym` (path with all `─ż_s` having the symmetry `sym`); `IsTrivialPhase sym H` = symmetry-connected to a product state (`IsProductStateHamiltonian`); `IsSPTPhase sym H` = short-range gapped-unique + `sym H` + ┬¼trivial. (8.3.6)ŌĆō(8.3.10)): the Haldane phase is protected by any of three symmetries ŌĆö (S1) ZŌéé├ŚZŌéé (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`), (S2) time-reversal (`IsTimeReversalInvariant` marker), (S3) bond-centered inversion (`IsBondInversionInvariant` marker). `vbsInversionParityS L S` (marker, Ōäż) + `tasaki_vbs_inversion_parity` (**AXIOM**): `├ø_inv|╬”_VBS^SŌ¤® = (ŌłÆ1)^{L┬ĘS}|╬”_VBS^SŌ¤®` ŌĆö odd `L┬ĘS` Ō¤╣ odd parity Ō¤╣ ZŌéé obstruction to connecting to the trivial state. `IsSpinSVBSNontrivialSPT S` (marker) + `tasaki_spt_classification` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` VBS is a nontrivial SPT phase **iff `S` is odd** (even `S` ŌćÆ trivial). `entanglementEntropyS` (marker, eqs. 8.3.7ŌĆō8.3.8): the bipartite entanglement entropy `ŌłÆ╬Ż p_j log p_j` from the Schmidt decomposition. ┬¦8.3.3 is heuristic; precise indices come in ┬¦8.3.4 (MPS) / ┬¦8.3.6 (Ogata) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTTopologicalIndex.lean` |

(8.3.42)ŌĆō(8.3.47)): the precise MPS invariant. A protecting symmetry `G` acts on the bond space by a projective representation with phase function (2-cocycle) `Žå : GŌåÆGŌåÆŌäØ` (`IsProjectiveRep` marker); it is **trivial** (`IsTrivialProjectiveRep` marker) iff `Žå` is a coboundary (eq. 8.3.43) ŌĆö the cohomology class is the SPT index. `SymmetricInjectiveMPSExists G Žå` (marker): an injective MPS invariant up to phase under `V╠é(g)`. `tasaki_theorem_8_7` (**AXIOM**): symmetric injective MPS Ō¤╣ trivial projective rep. For half-odd-integer spin (`N` odd), `z2z2Spin_nontrivial_of_odd` (**AXIOM**, eq. 2.1.31): the ZŌéé├ŚZŌéé rep is nontrivial. `tasaki_corollary_8_5` (**PROVED**, contrapositive of Thm 8.7): for `N` odd there is **no** ZŌéé├ŚZŌéé-invariant injective MPS ŌĆö the matrix-product LiebŌĆōSchultzŌĆōMattis no-go | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTMatrixProductIndex.lean` |

For **half-odd-integer** spin (`S=N/2`, `N` odd) with a short-ranged translation-invariant Hamiltonian (`HasShortRangeHamiltonianS`/`IsTranslationInvariantS` markers) having **either** ZŌéé├ŚZŌéé **or** time-reversal symmetry (`IsZ2Z2SymmetricS Ōł© IsTimeReversalSymmetricS`), the ground state can **never** be both unique and gapped: `tasaki_theorem_8_6` (**AXIOM**) `Ō¤╣ ┬¼HasUniqueGappedGroundStateS H`. (8.3.55)): the existence of a topological phase transition. `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS L s = s┬ĘakltHamiltonianS L + (1ŌłÆs)┬Ę╬Ż_x (┼£_x^{(3)})┬▓` (eq. 8.3.55) linearly connects the trivial Hamiltonian (8.1.2, `s=0`, large-`D` phase) to the AKLT/VBS Hamiltonian (7.1.1, `s=1`, Haldane phase), keeping all three protecting symmetries. `tasaki_theorem_8_8` (**AXIOM**): the model's infinite-volume limit undergoes a phase transition ŌĆö Ōłā intermediate `sŌłł(0,1)` at which the family `LŌå”─ż_s` is **either** gapless (`IsGaplessInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has multiple ground states (`HasMultipleGroundStatesInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has a local-expectation discontinuity (`HasLocalExpectationDiscontinuityInInfiniteVolume`) ŌĆö markers on the `LŌåæŌł×` family. (8.4.1), (8.4.19)): a gapped phase whose degeneracy is protected by topology, not symmetry. Qubits live on the edges `ToricEdge L = (ZMod L ├Ś ZMod L) ├Ś Fin 2` of an `L├ŚL` torus (`2L┬▓` edges); `starEdges`/`plaquetteEdges` are the 4-edge stars `A_v`/plaquettes `B_p`; `sigmaZS`/`sigmaXS` = `Žā^z`/`Žā^x`. `toricCodeHamiltonianS L = ŌłÆ╬Ż_v ŌłÅ_{A_v} Žā^z ŌłÆ ╬Ż_p ŌłÅ_{B_p} Žā^x` (eq. 8.4.1, commuting stabilizers, gap Ōēź 2, 4-fold degenerate on the torus). `perturbedToricHamiltonianS L ╬Ą V = ─ż_tc + ╬Ą ╬Ż_x V_x` (eq. 8.4.19). `tasaki_theorem_8_9` (**AXIOM**): for range-`r` perturbations there are **L-independent** `╬ĄŌéĆ,╬ö>0` such that for `|╬Ą|Ōēż╬ĄŌéĆ`, all `LŌēź2` (nontrivial torus), and any local perturbation (`IsToricPerturbation`), the perturbed Hamiltonian has **four near-degenerate ground states** separated by `╬ö` (`HasFourNearlyDegenerateGroundStates`) ŌĆö topological 4-fold degeneracy stable under arbitrary perturbation, no symmetry needed. Lattice + stabilizers + both Hamiltonians concrete | `Quantum/SpinS/ToricCode.lean` |

| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `ŌłÅ_{x Ōłł A} (-1)^(Žā x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V ŌåÆ Bool`, and configuration `Žā : V ŌåÆ Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |

Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬¦2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `ŌłÅ_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(Žā (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) ├Ś Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `ŌłÅ_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(Žā ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) ├Ś Fin (2L)) ├Ś Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig Žā) = (-1)^K ┬Ę marshallSignChainConfig K Žā`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)┬Ę(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K Žā ŌĆó basisVec Žā`; specialisation at the N├®el configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the N├®el state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* N├®el configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) ŌĆö direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* N├®el state coincides with the time-reversed N├®el state in 1D, 2D, 3D ŌĆö both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. | `marshallDressedBasis A Žā` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A Žā ŌĆó basisVec Žā` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V ŌåÆ Bool` (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). | `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `╬©╠ā^Žā Žā = marshallSignOf A Žā`; `╬©╠ā^Žā Žä = 0` for `Žä ŌēĀ Žā`; explicit `╬©╠ā^Žā Žä = marshallSignOf A Žā ┬Ę basisVec Žā Žä` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `┬▒1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A Žā)┬▓ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `╬Ż_Žä ╬©╠ā^Žā Žä ┬Ę ╬©╠ā^Žü Žä = if Žü = Žā then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{Žā╠ä/2}` as the underlying `basisVec Žā` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `╬Ż_x Žā_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `┼£_x ┬Ę ┼£_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) Žā Žā').im = 0` for all `x, y, Žā, Žā'`. | `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : ╬ø ŌåÆ ╬ø ŌåÆ Ōäé` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬Ż_{x,y} J(x,y) ┬Ę spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) Žā Žā').im = 0`. ŌäØ-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A Žā` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A Žā).im = 0`. Each factor of the product is `┬▒1 Ōłł ŌäØ` (either `1` or `(-1 : Ōäé)^(Žā x : ŌäĢ)` with `(Žā x : ŌäĢ) Ōłł {0, 1}`); products of reals are real | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `╬Ż_Žä \|╬©╠ā^ŽāŌ¤® Žä ┬Ę (H ┬Ę \|╬©╠ā^{Žā'}Ō¤®) Žä` is real (Tasaki ┬¦2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). | `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `╬Ż_Žä \|╬©╠ā^ŽāŌ¤® Žä ┬Ę (M ┬Ę \|╬©╠ā^{Žā'}Ō¤®) Žä = marshallSignOf A Žā ┬Ę marshallSignOf A Žā' ┬Ę M Žā Žā'`. | `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x ŌēĀ A y`) with `Žā` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`Žā x ŌēĀ Žā y`), `marshallSignOf A Žā * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap Žā x y) = -1`. The combined product over `╬ø` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(┬▒1)┬▓ = 1`; at the unique site in `A Ōł® {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(Žā x + Žā y) = -1` since `Žā x ŌēĀ Žā y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `Žā ŌēĀ Žā'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A Žā ┬Ę marshallSignOf A Žā' ┬Ę J(x,y) ┬Ę (spinHalfDot x y) Žā Žā'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. | `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `Žā ŌēĀ Žā'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `╬Ż_Žä \|╬©╠ā^ŽāŌ¤® Žä ┬Ę (H ┬Ę \|╬©╠ā^{Žā'}Ō¤®) Žä` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. | `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `Žā Ōå” basisSwap Žā x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `Žā x ŌēĀ Žā y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `Žā x ŌēĀ Žā y`, `SwapReachable G Žā (basisSwap Žā x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `Žā z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `Žā x ŌēĀ Žā y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 p. 41) ŌĆö combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives PerronŌĆōFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `HŌéĆIndex ╬ø` | index type `{Žā : ╬ø ŌåÆ Fin 2 // magnetization ╬ø Žā = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `HŌéĆIndex ╬ø` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A Žā ┬Ę marshallSignOf A Žä ┬Ę (H_J)_{Žā,Žä})` ŌĆö the matrix to which Tasaki's PerronŌĆōFrobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `Žā ŌēĀ Žä`, packaged from PR ╬▒-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap Žā x y` preserves total magnetisation. Proof uses the identification `basisSwap Žā x y = Žā Ōłś Equiv.swap x y` (the swap is a permutation of `╬ø`); the magnetisation `Ōłæ_z spinSign(Žā z)` is invariant under such reindexing (`Equiv.sum_comp`). | `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `Žā` and `Žā'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `Žā ŌēĀ Žā'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `Žā x = 0, Žā' x = 1`) and `y` (with `Žā y = 1, Žā' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `Žā ŌēĀ Žā'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x Ōłł D01`, `y Ōłł D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `Žā'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `Žā'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬¦2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `Žā`, `Žā'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `Ōēź 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for PerronŌĆōFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := c┬ĘI ŌłÆ M` on `HŌéĆIndex ╬ø`. Used as input to PerronŌĆōFrobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c Ōēź M Žā Žā` for all `Žā`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) Žā (basisSwap Žā x y) = 1/2` for `x ŌēĀ y` and antiparallel `Žā_x ŌēĀ Žā_y`. | `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x ŌēĀ y`, `Žā_x ŌēĀ Žā_y`, and `(u, v) Ōłē {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap Žā x y) u v ŌēĀ Žā`. Site analysis: `Žā` and `Žā' = basisSwap Žā x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `Žā`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. | `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `Žā' = basisSwap Žā x y` (with `x ŌēĀ y`, `Žā_x ŌēĀ Žā_y`) and any `(u, v) Ōłē {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) Žā Žā' = 0`. | `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) Žā (basisSwap Žā x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. | `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick ├Ś Heisenberg formula ├Ś symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B Žā Žä` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. | `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. | `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) Žā Žä > 0` and `B Žä Žü > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) Žā Žü > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `Žā : HŌéĆIndex ╬ø` and any `╬Š` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) Žā.val ╬Š`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) Žā Ō¤©╬Š, h_magŌ¤® > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using ╬▒-5j (single-step swap positivity) and ╬▒-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ┬Ę I ŌłÆ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M Žā Žā` (strict). | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR ╬▒-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronŌĆōFrobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **PerronŌĆōFrobenius applied to `B = c ┬Ę I ŌłÆ M` on `H_0`**: existence of a strictly positive eigenvector `v` for some real eigenvalue `╬╝`, and uniqueness up to positive scalar. Translating back to `M`, `v` is the eigenvector for the **minimum** eigenvalue (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg). This is the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4): the H_0 ground-state expansion `╬Ż_Žā c_Žā \|╬©╠ā^ŽāŌ¤®` with `c_Žā = v Žā > 0` is unique up to positive scalar | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `bipartiteCoupling` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian` | the Tasaki ┬¦2.5 p. 40 toy Hamiltonian setup: `bipartiteCoupling A x y := if A x ŌēĀ A y then 1 else 0` (the unnormalised bipartite coupling), and `heisenbergToyHamiltonian A := heisenbergHamiltonian (bipartiteCoupling A)`. Real symmetric, non-negative, supported on bipartite bonds, positive on inter-sublattice pairs. Hermitian. | `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `╬ø` from sublattice indicator `A : ╬ø ŌåÆ Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x ŌēĀ A y`. | `bipartiteGraphFromA_preconnected` | `bipartiteGraphFromA A` is preconnected when both sublattices are non-empty. Provides the `G.Preconnected` hypothesis needed for MLM application to the toy Hamiltonian | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_toy_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **Matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) for the toy Hamiltonian**: the shifted toy matrix `B_toy = c ┬Ę I ŌłÆ M_toy` (under both-sublattices-nonempty + diagonal-shift hypothesis) has a unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive eigenvector. Specialises ╬▒-5o to the toy via ╬▒-6a + ╬▒-6b | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyPF.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp1/2/3` | sublattice spin operators `┼£_A^(╬▒) := ╬Ż_{x Ōłł A} onSite x ┼£^(╬▒)` for `╬▒ Ōłł {1, 2, 3}`. Foundation for the Casimir identity `─ż_toy = (1/(2|╬ø|))((┼£_tot)┬▓ ŌłÆ (┼£_A)┬▓ ŌłÆ (┼£_B)┬▓)` (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |

| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_of_cardNotA_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_totalSpinSSquared_of_cardNotA_zero` | **sublattice Ōåö total spin identities at the saturated edge case**: when `\|┬¼A\| = 0`, the complement sublattice `┬¼A` is empty, so `┼£_┬¼A^(╬▒) = 0` and `┼£_┬¼A┬▓ = 0`, and the `A`-sublattice operators coincide with the total operators: `┼£_A^(╬▒) = ┼£_tot^(╬▒)` for `╬▒ Ōłł {1,2,3}` and `┼£_A┬▓ = ┼£_tot┬▓`. Foundation for the dim-bound on the predicted GS subspace in the saturated case: `eigenspace(┼£_tot┬▓)_╬▒ ŌŖå bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` whenever `\|┬¼A\| = 0` and `╬▒ = s_A(s_A+1)` (PR #2784, file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeEqTotalOfCardZero.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquaredEigenspace_le_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **eigenspace bridge for the saturated case**: when `\|┬¼A\| = 0`, `eigenspace((┼£_tot)┬▓)_{s_A(s_A+1)} ŌŖå bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N`. Proof: any `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `s_A(s_A+1)` automatically satisfies all three joint Casimir eigenspace conditions, since (i) `(┼£_tot)┬▓` eigenvalue matches the predicted target `(s_AŌłÆ0)(s_AŌłÆ0+1)`, (ii) `┼£_A┬▓ = ┼£_tot┬▓` (PR #2784) gives the `(┼£_A)┬▓` condition for free, (iii) `┼£_┬¼A┬▓ = 0` trivially. Combined with PR #2768's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace = span(ladderIterateUp)` and finrank `2 m_max + 1`, this yields the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS) Ōēź 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` at the saturated edge case, matching the Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction `2 S_tot + 1` (PR #2785, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSEigenspaceBridge.lean`) |
| `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace_le_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **┬¦2.4 Ōåö ╬│-4 bridge at saturated case**: when `\|┬¼A\| = 0`, `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace J N ŌŖå bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` for any choice of coupling `J`. Direct corollary of PR #2785's eigenspace bridge applied to the `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenspace component of the saturated FM joint eigenspace, with the identification `m_max = s_A` (forced by `\|V\| = \|A\|`). With PR #2768 (joint = span ladder) and PR #2769 (finrank = 2 m_max + 1), this gives the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS subspace) Ōēź 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` (PR #2786, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedFMJointLePredictedGS.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardNotA_zero` | **predicted GS subspace dim Ōēź 2 m_max + 1 at saturated case**: when `\|┬¼A\| = 0`, `\|V\|┬ĘN + 1 Ōēż finrank(bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N)`. Direct corollary of PR #2786's bridge + PR #2769's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace_finrank_eq` via `Submodule.finrank_mono`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardA_zero` | **opposite saturated case**: when `\|A\| = 0`, the **complement-orientation** predicted GS subspace `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted (┬¼A) N` has `finrank Ōēź \|V\|┬ĘN + 1`. Note: with `\|A\| = 0`, the original-orientation predicted GS subspace would target a different (┼£_tot)┬▓ eigenvalue (signed-difference goes negative); the complement orientation gives the natural choice (PR #2788, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSFinrankOppositeSaturated.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eigenspace_at_predicted_min_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardNotA_zero` | **─ż_toy_S eigenspace finrank lower bound at saturated case**: when `\|┬¼A\| = 0`, the `─ż_toy_S`-eigenspace at `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N` has finrank `Ōēź \|V\|┬ĘN + 1`. From the bilinear expansion (PR #1058) plus diagonal `┼£_x ┬Ę ┼£_x = N(N+2)/4 ┬Ę 1` and off-diagonal `┼£_x ┬Ę ┼£_y ┬Ę \|Žā_ŌŖżŌ¤® = N┬▓/4 ┬Ę \|Žā_ŌŖżŌ¤®` for `x ŌēĀ y`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on the all-up state**: `─ż_toy_S ┬Ę \|Žā_ŌŖżŌ¤® = ((\|╬ø\|┬ĘN/2)(\|╬ø\|┬ĘN/2+1) ŌłÆ (\|A\|┬ĘN/2)(\|A\|┬ĘN/2+1) ŌłÆ (\|┬¼A\|┬ĘN/2)(\|┬¼A\|┬ĘN/2+1)) ┬Ę \|Žā_ŌŖżŌ¤®`. Algebraic simplification of PR #1060 via `\|╬ø\| = \|A\| + \|┬¼A\|` and the identity `(a+b)(a+b+1) ŌłÆ a(a+1) ŌłÆ b(b+1) = 2ab`. Specialises to spin-`1/2` eigenvalue `\|A\|┬Ę\|┬¼A\|/2`.
