| `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp3_variance_eq_zero` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_totalSpinHalfOp3_variance_eq_zero` | **`┼£_tot^(3)` zero variance on basis states**: `<(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓> ŌłÆ <┼£_tot^(3)>┬▓ = 0` on `basisVecS Žā` (spin-`S`) and on `basisVec Žā` (spin-`1/2`). Direct corollary of steps 205/206 (spin-`S`) and 204/207 (spin-`1/2`); witnesses that every basis state is a sharp `┼£_tot^(3)`-eigenstate (╬│-4 step 208) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1255) |
| `basisVecS_off_diagonal_totalSpinSOp3` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_off_diagonal_totalSpinHalfOp3` | **Off-diagonal `┼£_tot^(3)` matrix elements vanish**: `<basisVecS Žä \| ┼£_tot^(3) \| basisVecS Žā> = 0` for `Žä ŌēĀ Žā` (spin-`S`), and analogue for spin-`1/2`. Witnesses that `┼£_tot^(3)` is diagonal in the computational basis (╬│-4 step 209) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1256) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_basisVecS_of_ne` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_basisVec_of_ne` | **Basis orthogonality**: `<basisVecS Žä \| basisVecS Žā> = 0` for `Žä ŌēĀ Žā` (spin-`S`), and analogue for spin-`1/2`. Companion to the existing diagonal `_inner_self` (= 1) lemma; together they witness that the computational basis is orthonormal (╬│-4 step 210) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1257) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_basisVec` | **Combined ╬┤-form**: `<basisVecS Žä \| basisVecS Žā> = if Žä = Žā then 1 else 0` (and spin-`1/2` analogue). Single-statement combination of the diagonal-1 and off-diagonal-0 facts (╬│-4 step 211) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1258) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_totalSpinSOp3_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_totalSpinHalfOp3_basisVec` | **Combined `┼£_tot^(3)` ╬┤-form matrix element**: `<basisVecS Žä \| ┼£_tot^(3) \| basisVecS Žā> = if Žä = Žā then magEigenvalueS Žā else 0` (spin-`S`); spin-`1/2` analogue with `M(Žā)/2`. Single-statement combination of ╬│-4 step 206/207 (diagonal) and ╬│-4 step 209 (off-diagonal) (╬│-4 step 212) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1259) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_totalSpinSOp3_sq_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_basisVec` | **Combined `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` ╬┤-form matrix element**: `<basisVecS Žä \| (┼£_tot^(3))┬▓ \| basisVecS Žā> = if Žä = Žā then (magEigenvalueS Žā)┬▓ else 0` (spin-`S`); spin-`1/2` analogue with `(M(Žā)/2)┬▓`. Off-diagonal proven via the `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvector identity applied twice + basis orthogonality (╬│-4 step 213) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1260) |
| `basisVecS_expectation_totalSpinSOp1` / `_totalSpinSOp2` | **Spin-`S` transverse `┼£_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVecS Žā \| ┼£_tot^(1) \| basisVecS Žā> = 0` and `<basisVecS Žā \| ┼£_tot^(2) \| basisVecS Žā> = 0`. Direct from per-site `basisVecS_expectation_onSiteS_spinSOp{1,2}` summed over sites via `Matrix.sum_mulVec` + `dotProduct_sum` (╬│-4 step 214) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleSiteTransverseMeanZero.lean` (PR #1261) |
| `basisVec_expectation_totalSpinHalfOp1` / `_totalSpinHalfOp2` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse `┼£_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVec Žā \| ┼£_tot^(1) \| basisVec Žā> = 0` and `<basisVec Žā \| ┼£_tot^(2) \| basisVec Žā> = 0`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 214; per-site uses `basisVec_expectation_eq_diagonal` + `pauliX/Y` zero diagonal (╬│-4 step 215) | `Quantum/SpinDot/Hamiltonian.lean` (PR #1262) |
| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼£_tot)┬▓` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVec Žā`**: `<basisVec Žā \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| basisVec Žā> = \|╬ø\|/2 + (M(Žā)/2)┬▓` for any `Žā : ╬ø ŌåÆ Fin 2`. Generalises ╬│-4 step 201 (constant case has `M = ┬▒\|╬ø\|` giving `\|╬ø\|┬Ę(\|╬ø\|+2)/4`). Proof: reduce to diagonal matrix element, expand `(┼£_tot)┬▓ = ╬Ż_{x,y} spinHalfDot x y`, unify per-pair diagonal as `m_x m_y/4 + (1/2)┬Ę[x=y]`, then split via separability (╬│-4 step 216) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1263) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` `(┼£_tot)┬▓` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVecS Žā`**: `<basisVecS Žā \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| basisVecS Žā> = \|V\|┬ĘN(N+2)/4 + magEigenvalueS(Žā)┬▓ ŌłÆ ╬Ż_x (N/2 ŌłÆ Žāx.val)┬▓` for any `Žā : ╬ø ŌåÆ Fin (N + 1)`. Spin-`S` analogue of ╬│-4 step 216; the per-site sum-of-squares term is the configuration-dependent z-axis squared contribution that doesn't collapse for spin-`S`. Spin-`1/2` is the special case where the term reduces to constant `\|╬ø\|/4` (╬│-4 step 218) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1265) |
| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir `(┼£_tot^(1))┬▓ + (┼£_tot^(2))┬▓` on arbitrary `basisVec Žā` is constant `\|╬ø\|/2`**: independent of `Žā`. Direct corollary of ╬│-4 step 216 + ╬│-4 step 204 (z-axis cancels). Expresses the per-state transverse contribution as a pure combinatorial quantity (╬│-4 step 219) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1266) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼£_tot^(1))┬▓ + (┼£_tot^(2))┬▓` on arbitrary `basisVecS Žā` = `\|V\|┬ĘN(N+2)/4 ŌłÆ ╬Ż_x (N/2 ŌłÆ Žāx.val)┬▓`**. Direct corollary of ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 205 (`magEigenvalueS┬▓` cancels). Reduces to `\|V\|┬ĘN/2` for N├®el on balanced bipartite graphs (matches ╬│-4 step 156) and to `\|╬ø\|/2` for spin-`1/2` (╬│-4 step 220) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1267) |
| `neelConfigOfS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **N├®el per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬Ż_x (N/2 ŌłÆ (neelConfigOfS A N x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬Ę(N/2)┬▓`. Both extremes `Žāx Ōłł {0, N}` give `(N/2)┬▓`. Bridges ╬│-4 step 218 (general formula) to existing closed-form N├®el Casimir (╬│-4 step 221) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1268) |
| `allAlignedConfigS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **AllAligned per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬Ż_x (N/2 ŌłÆ (allAlignedConfigS V N c x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬Ę(N/2 ŌłÆ c.val)┬▓`. Trivial direct computation since `Žāx = c` constant. Bridges ╬│-4 step 218 to existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_/_last_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (╬│-4 step 222) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` (PR #1269) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS` | **N├®el `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = (\|A\| ŌłÆ \|┬¼A\|)┬ĘN/2`. Direct from `magSumS_neelConfigOfS` (= `\|┬¼A\|┬ĘN`) and the filter-card decomposition `\|V\| = \|A\| + \|┬¼A\|`. Named lemma extracted from inlined computation in `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_card_Lambda` (╬│-4 step 224) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1271) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero/_last_totalSpinSSquared_via_general` | **Spin-`S` allAligned-up/down Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬”_ŌŖż \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| ╬”_ŌŖż> = m_max┬Ę(m_max+1)` (and same for `╬”_ŌŖź`) where `m_max = \|V\|┬ĘN/2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 222 + `magEigenvalueS_allAlignedConfigS` + `ring`. Recovers existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (╬│-4 step 226) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1273) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` N├®el Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬”_N├®el \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| ╬”_N├®el> = ((\|A\|ŌłÆ\|┬¼A\|)/2)┬▓ + \|╬ø\|/2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 216 + `magnetization_neelConfigOf` + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 225 (╬│-4 step 227) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1274) |
| `basisVec_const_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` const-state Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<basisVec(const s) \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| basisVec(const s)> = \|╬ø\|┬Ę(\|╬ø\|+2)/4` for any `s : Fin 2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 216 + `magnetization (const s) = ┬▒\|╬ø\|` + `(spinSign s)┬▓ = 1` + `ring`. Recovers existing ╬│-4 step 201 (╬│-4 step 228) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1275) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement` | **Complement N├®el `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬¼A) N) = (\|┬¼A\|ŌłÆ\|A\|)┬ĘN/2`. Sublattice-swap symmetry; complement of ╬│-4 step 224. Direct corollary by applying ╬│-4 step 224 to `(┬¼A)` (╬│-4 step 229) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1276) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_simplified` | **Cleaner form** of ╬│-4 step 229 with double negation `!!A` simplified to `A` via `Finset.filter_congr` + `Bool.not_not`: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬¼A) N) = (\|┬¼A\|ŌłÆ\|A\|)┬ĘN/2` (╬│-4 step 230) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1277) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_eq_neg` | **Negation relation**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬¼A) N) = ŌłÆmagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 224 + ╬│-4 step 230 + `ring`. The complement N├®el sits at the opposite `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvalue (╬│-4 step 231) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1278) |
| `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization ╬ø (neelConfigOf (┬¼A)) = ŌłÆmagnetization ╬ø (neelConfigOf A)`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 231; direct from existing `magnetization_neelConfigOf{,_complement}` + `ring` (╬│-4 step 232) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1279) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬”_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)┬▓+\|╬ø\|/2`) + ╬│-4 step 232 (`M(┬¼A) = ŌłÆM(A)`): `(M)┬▓` is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬”_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS┬▓` is sign-flip invariant; `╬Ż_x m_x┬▓` evaluates to `\|V\|┬Ę(N/2)┬▓` for both A and ┬¼A. Spin-`S` mirror of ╬│-4 step 233 (╬│-4 step 234) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1281) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬”_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 205 + 231 + `ring`: squared eigenvalue is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 235) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1282) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬”_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬”_N├®el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 235 (╬│-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼£^1)┬▓+(┼£^2)┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: equal expectations on `╬”_N├®el(A)` and `╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|┬ĘN/2` via ╬│-4 step 220 + 221 (╬│-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N├®el**: both expectations equal `\|╬ø\|/2`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 219 (Žā-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 237 (╬│-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `┼£_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├®el**: `<╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬”_N├®el(┬¼A)> = ŌłÆ<╬”_N├®el(A)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬”_N├®el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 206 + 231 (╬│-4 step 239) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1286) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `┼£_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├®el**. Direct from ╬│-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 239 (╬│-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N├®el transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<╬”_N├®el\|┼£_tot^(1,2)\|╬”_N├®el> = 0`. Trivial corollary of ╬│-4 step 214 applied to `Žā = neelConfigOfS A N` (╬│-4 step 241). Together with ╬│-4 step 91 (`<┼£_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|ŌłÆ\|┬¼A\|)┬ĘN/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N├®el transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as ╬│-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from ╬│-4 step 215 (╬│-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ╬Ż_{j=1}^z ┼£_0 ┬Ę ┼£_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `ŌłÆS(1 + zS)` (╬│-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j Ōłł univ.erase 0` (╬│-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = Ōłģ` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `ŌłÆS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z Ōēź 1` (╬│-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬”_ŌŖż\|H\|╬”_ŌŖż> = z┬Ę(N/2)┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `ŌłÆS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (╬│-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<╬”_aligned(c)\|H\|╬”_aligned(c)> = z┬Ę(N/2 ŌłÆ c.val)┬▓`. Generalises ╬│-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z┬Ę(N/2)┬▓` (╬│-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<╬”_ŌŖź\|H\|╬”_ŌŖź> = z┬Ę(N/2)┬▓`. Direct specialisation of ╬│-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (╬│-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `┼£_R^(╬▒) = ╬Ż_{j ŌēĀ 0} onSiteS j ┼£^(╬▒)` for axes `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `┼£^(╬▒)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (┼£_tot┬▓ ŌłÆ ┼£_0┬▓ ŌłÆ ┼£_R┬▓)/2` (╬│-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`┼£_0 ┬Ę ┼£_R` decomposition**: `H = ╬Ż_╬▒ onSiteS 0 (┼£^(╬▒)) ┬Ę leafSpinSOp_╬▒`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `╬Ż_j (A * B_j) = A * (╬Ż_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (╬│-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_╬▒ (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (┼£^(╬▒)) + leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (╬│-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `┼£_R┬▓ := (┼£_R^(1))┬▓ + (┼£_R^(2))┬▓ + (┼£_R^(3))┬▓`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (╬│-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1299) |

Provides the **third pairwise commutativity** needed for the joint eigenbasis of `(┼£_tot)┬▓`, `(┼£_A)┬▓`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` (the first two are ╬▒-6r self-invariance and ╬▒-6o inter-Casimir cross-commute) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot` / `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_isHermitian` | cross-sublattice spin dot product: `┼£_A ┬Ę ┼£_B := ╬Ż_╬▒ ┼£_A^(╬▒) ┼£_B^(╬▒)`. `┼£_A ┬Ę ┼£_┬¼A` is Hermitian (each summand is the product of two commuting Hermitian operators). Bilinear expansion `sublatticeSpinDot_eq_sum_sum`: `┼£_A ┬Ę ┼£_B = ╬Ż_{x : A x} ╬Ż_{y : B y} ┼£_x ┬Ę ┼£_y` connects the operator-level Casimir form to the bond-level Heisenberg expression in the toy Hamiltonian (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 (2.5.10)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` / `_of_const_on` | `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ╬Ż_{x Ōłł A} ╬Ż_{y Ōłł A} ┼£_x ┬Ę ┼£_y` (specialisation `B = A` of the bilinear expansion), and the **maximum-spin Casimir eigenvalue on the all-aligned state**: `(┼£_A)┬▓ ┬Ę \|s s ŌĆ” sŌ¤® = (\|A\|┬Ę(\|A\|+2)/4) ┬Ę \|s s ŌĆ” sŌ¤®` for any `s : Fin 2`. Generalised form `_of_const_on`: any `\|ŽāŌ¤®` with `Žā` **constant on `A`** is an eigenvector with eigenvalue `\|A\|┬Ę(\|A\|+2)/4` (relevant for N├®el-style states which are constant on each sublattice but not globally) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_sublatticeSpinDot_sum` | the MLM toy Hamiltonian decomposes as an oriented cross-sublattice spin dot product: `─ż_toy = ┼£_A ┬Ę ┼£_┬¼A + ┼£_┬¼A ┬Ę ┼£_A`. Bridges the bipartite-bond sum (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 (2.5.10)) to the operator-level Casimir form (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_two_sublatticeSpinDot` | cross-sublattice symmetry: `┼£_A ┬Ę ┼£_┬¼A = ┼£_┬¼A ┬Ę ┼£_A` (each axis pair commutes by `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute`), giving the closed form `─ż_toy = 2 ŌĆó ┼£_A ┬Ę ┼£_┬¼A` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir identity** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 (2.5.11)): `(┼£_tot)┬▓ = (┼£_A)┬▓ + 2 ŌĆó (┼£_A ┬Ę ┼£_┬¼A) + (┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` (per-axis `(a + b)┬▓ = a┬▓ + 2ab + b┬▓` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/|╬ø|`) `─ż_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ŌłÆ (┼£_A)┬▓ ŌłÆ (┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(┼£_tot)┬▓` (specialisation of `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared`). The standard fact used to project the toy ground state onto a fixed `(┼£_tot)┬▓` eigenspace, underpinning the `S_tot = 0` selection of the toy ground state | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `_complement` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(┼£_A)┬▓` and with `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`. Direct consequences of the closed form `─ż_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ŌłÆ (┼£_A)┬▓ ŌłÆ (┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` and the three pairwise Casimir commutativities (PRs ╬▒-6o, ╬▒-6s, self-commute trivially). Together with ╬▒-6p, gives all four Casimir-style commutators of `─ż_toy`, the prerequisite for the joint eigenbasis analysis on which the `S_tot = 0` selection rests | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_simplified` | explicit eigenvalue of `─ż_toy` on the all-aligned state: the Casimir-difference form `\|╬ø\|┬Ę(\|╬ø\|+2)/4 ŌłÆ \|A\|┬Ę(\|A\|+2)/4 ŌłÆ \|┬¼A\|┬Ę(\|┬¼A\|+2)/4` algebraically simplifies via `\|╬ø\| = \|A\| + \|┬¼A\|` to the **product form** `\|A\|┬Ę\|┬¼A\|/2`. The eigenvalue is non-negative for any bipartite lattice and strictly positive when both sublattices are non-empty | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_neelStateOf` / `_complement_mulVec_neelStateOf` | sublattice Casimir eigenvalues on the **N├®el state** `╬”_N├®el(A) := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`: `(┼£_A)┬▓ ┬Ę \|╬”_N├®el(A)Ō¤® = (\|A\|┬Ę(\|A\|+2)/4) ┬Ę \|╬”_N├®el(A)Ō¤®` and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓ ┬Ę \|╬”_N├®el(A)Ō¤® = (\|┬¼A\|┬Ę(\|┬¼A\|+2)/4) ┬Ę \|╬”_N├®el(A)Ō¤®`. Direct corollaries of `_of_const_on` since the N├®el configuration is constant on each sublattice (`Žā x = 0` on `A`, `Žā x = 1` on `┬¼A`); the N├®el state is simultaneously a `(┼£_A)┬▓` and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` eigenvector at maximum-spin eigenvalues | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelCore.lean` |
| `mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_commute_of_mem` | generic preservation: any operator `A` with `Commute A (┼£tot^(3))` maps each magnetisation sector `H_M` to itself ŌĆö operator-level form of Tasaki ┬¦2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 block-diagonalisation | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | Casimir specialisation: `┼£tot┬▓` preserves every `H_M` (since `[┼£tot┬▓, ┼£tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : ╬ø ŌåÆ ╬ø ŌåÆ Ōäé` and `M : Ōäé`, `v Ōłł H_M` implies `H ┬Ę v Ōłł H_M` ŌĆö the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki ┬¦2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, ┼£tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : Ōäé`, `v Ōłł H_M` implies `┼£tot^- ┬Ę v Ōłł H_{M-1}` ŌĆö the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[┼£tot^(3), ┼£tot^-] = -┼£tot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : Ōäé`, `v Ōłł H_M` implies `┼£tot^+ ┬Ę v Ōłł H_{M+1}` ŌĆö the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[┼£tot^(3), ┼£tot^+] = +┼£tot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `╬ø = Fin 2` and any `╬Ė`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė) * onSite 1 (├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė)` (general-╬Ė extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` ŌĆö bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_eq_exp` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.11): `├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė_tot = exp(-i╬Ė ┼£_tot^(╬▒))`. Composes `spinHalfRot{╬▒}_eq_exp` (single site), `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` (per-site bridge), and `Matrix.exp_sum_of_commute` (commuting-summand exp = noncommProd of exps) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki ┬¦2.2 (2.2.12) ŌåÆ (2.2.13): `Commute A (┼£_tot^(╬▒)) ŌåÆ Commute A (├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `├ø` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (┼£^┬▒_tot) ŌåÆ Commute A (exp(c ŌĆó ┼£^┬▒_tot))` for any `c : Ōäé` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė_tot)ß┤┤ * ├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-i╬Ė ┼£_tot^(╬▒)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (┼£_tot^(╬▒)) ŌåÆ (├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė_tot)ß┤┤ * A * ├ø^(╬▒)_╬Ė_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

| `basisVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | `|ŽāŌ¤® Ōłł H_{|Žā|/2}` ŌĆö basis states lie in their magnetization subspace | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_disjoint` | distinct sectors `H_M ŌŖō H_{M'} = ŌŖź` (`M ŌēĀ M'`) ŌĆö eigenvalue uniqueness | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `iSup_magnetizationSubspace_eq_top` | `Ō©å_M H_M = ŌŖż` ŌĆö every vector decomposes as a sum across sectors | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki ┬¦2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬¦2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |sŌĆ”sŌ¤® = (╬Ż_{x,y} J(x,y)┬Ę(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) ┬Ę |sŌĆ”sŌ¤®` ŌĆö every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki ┬¦2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-┼£_x┬Ę┼£_y |╬”ŌåæŌ¤® = -S┬▓ |╬”ŌåæŌ¤®` for `S = 1/2`, `x ŌēĀ y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

(2.4.6) (`|╬×_╬Ė,ŽĢŌ¤®` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `┼£_tot┬▓ (|ŌåæŌåōŌ¤® - |ŌåōŌåæŌ¤®) = 0` for `╬ø = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `┼£_tot┬▓ (|ŌåæŌåōŌ¤® + |ŌåōŌåæŌ¤®) = 2(|ŌåæŌåōŌ¤® + |ŌåōŌåæŌ¤®)` for `╬ø = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `┼£_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x S╬▒, ╬Ż_z onSite z S╬▓] = onSite x [S╬▒, S╬▓]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬¦3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `Žā = 2┬ĘS` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ╬Ż Žā^z_i Žā^z_{i+1} - h ╬Ż Žā^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `Žā^z_x ┬Ę Žā^z_y` (generic in `╬ø`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : ╬ø ŌåÆ ╬ø ŌåÆ Ōäé) (h : Ōäé)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `╬Ż J(x,y) Žā^z_x Žā^z_y ŌłÆ h ╬Ż Žā^x_x` for any finite `╬ø` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ╬▓ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `╬Ż_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `╬Ż_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i Ōå” i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N├®el state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬¦2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 ŌēĪ 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |

| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(Ō¤©H_Ising ┬Ę OŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(Ō¤©H_Ising^2Ō¤®_╬▓).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(Ō¤©H_Ising^nŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` for any `n : ŌäĢ` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Ō¤©A┬ĘB + B┬ĘAŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Ō¤©A┬ĘB ŌłÆ B┬ĘAŌ¤®_╬▓).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_╬▓(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ╬▓ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓).re : Ōäé) = Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(Žü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `Ō¤©H_IsingŌ¤®_╬▓ = -J ┬Ę Ōłæ Ō¤©Žā^z Žā^zŌ¤®_╬▓ + (-h) ┬Ę Ōłæ Ō¤©Žā^xŌ¤®_╬▓` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ┬¦3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬¦3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ╬▓ H` | `exp(-╬▓H) := Matrix.exp (-╬▓ ŌĆó H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0┬ĘH) = 1` (Tasaki ┬¦3.3, pp. 75ŌĆō78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(╬▓Ōéü+╬▓Ōéé)H) = exp(-╬▓ŌéüH) ┬Ę exp(-╬▓ŌééH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-╬▓(HŌéü+HŌéé)) = exp(-╬▓HŌéü) ┬Ę exp(-╬▓HŌéé)` for commuting `HŌéü, HŌéé` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(╬▓H) ┬Ę exp(-╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-╬▓H) ┬Ę exp(╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-╬▓H) ŌēĀ 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `Žü_╬▓ ŌēĀ 0` when `Z(╬▓) ŌēĀ 0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(Žü_╬▓)Ōü╗┬╣ = Z(╬▓) ┬Ę e^{╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) ŌēĀ 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(╬▓) ┬Ę Žü_╬▓ = e^{-╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) ŌēĀ 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(╬▓) ┬Ę Ō¤©AŌ¤®_╬▓ = Tr(e^{-╬▓H} ┬Ę A)` when `Z(╬▓) ŌēĀ 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬Ę╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : ŌäĢ` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2╬▓)H) = exp(-╬▓H) ┬Ę exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-╬▓H))Ōü╗┬╣ = exp(╬▓H)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬Ę╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : Ōäż` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn ╬▓ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-╬▓H))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (╬ø ŌåÆ Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) ŌēĀ 0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n Ōäé`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn ╬▓ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(Žü_╬▓┬▓) = Z(2╬▓) / Z(╬▓)┬▓` (purity / R├®nyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(Žü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for any `n : ŌäĢ` (R├®nyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `Žü_0 = (1/dim) ┬Ę I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `Žü_0Ōü╗┬╣ = dim ┬Ę I` (matrix inverse at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `Ō¤©AŌ¤®_0 = (1/dim) ┬Ę Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState ╬▓ H` | `Žü := (1/Z) ŌĆó exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(Žü) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `Žü` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation ╬▓ H O` | `Ō¤©OŌ¤® := Matrix.trace (Žü * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `Ō¤©1Ō¤® = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `Ō¤©OŌéü + OŌééŌ¤® = Ō¤©OŌéüŌ¤® + Ō¤©OŌééŌ¤®` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `Ō¤©c ┬Ę OŌ¤® = c ┬Ę Ō¤©OŌ¤®` (scalar linearity, `c : Ōäé`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `Ō¤©-OŌ¤® = -Ō¤©OŌ¤®` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `Ō¤©A - BŌ¤® = Ō¤©AŌ¤® - Ō¤©BŌ¤®` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `Ō¤©Ōłæ i Ōłł s, f iŌ¤® = Ōłæ i Ōłł s, Ō¤©f iŌ¤®` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-╬▓H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬¦3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[Žü_╬▓, H] = 0`, i.e. `Žü_╬▓` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki ┬¦3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A ┬Ę B)) = Tr(A ┬Ę B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n Ōäé` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓ = Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓).re : Ōäé) = Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `Ō¤©H ┬Ę AŌ¤®_╬▓ = Ō¤©A ┬Ę HŌ¤®_╬▓` for any `A` (Tasaki ┬¦3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `Ō¤©A ┬Ę OŌ¤®_╬▓ = Ō¤©O ┬Ę AŌ¤®_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `Ō¤©A ┬Ę O ŌłÆ O ┬Ę AŌ¤®_╬▓ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `Ō¤©[H, A]Ō¤®_╬▓ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(Ō¤©HŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Ō¤©O ┬Ę OŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Ō¤©O^nŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : ŌäĢ` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance ╬▓ H O` | `Var_╬▓(O) := Ō¤©O ┬Ę OŌ¤®_╬▓ ŌłÆ Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓┬▓` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_╬▓(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at ╬▓ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) ┬Ę Tr(O┬▓) ŌłÆ ((1/dim) ┬Ę Tr O)┬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_╬▓(O) = Ō¤©(O ŌłÆ Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓ ┬Ę 1) ┬Ę (O ŌłÆ Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓ ┬Ę 1)Ō¤®_╬▓` (centered-square form, `Z ŌēĀ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) := Ō¤©A ┬Ę BŌ¤®_╬▓ ŌłÆ Ō¤©AŌ¤®_╬▓ ┬Ę Ō¤©BŌ¤®_╬▓` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) ŌłÆ Cov_╬▓(B, A) = Ō¤©A ┬Ę B ŌłÆ B ┬Ę AŌ¤®_╬▓` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_╬▓(AŌéü + AŌéé, B) = Cov_╬▓(AŌéü, B) + Cov_╬▓(AŌéé, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, BŌéü + BŌéé) = Cov_╬▓(A, BŌéü) + Cov_╬▓(A, BŌéé)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_╬▓(c ŌĆó A, B) = c ┬Ę Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, c ŌĆó B) = c ┬Ę Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_╬▓(c ŌĆó 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_╬▓(A, c ŌĆó 1) = 0` (when `Z ŌēĀ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_╬▓(c ŌĆó 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_╬▓(A, c ŌĆó 1) = 0` (when `Z ŌēĀ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) := (1/2) ┬Ę Ō¤©A ┬Ę B + B ┬Ę AŌ¤®_╬▓ ŌłÆ Ō¤©AŌ¤®_╬▓ ┬Ę Ō¤©BŌ¤®_╬▓` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_╬▓` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_╬▓(A + B) = Var_╬▓(A) + Var_╬▓(B) + 2 ┬Ę Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_╬▓(1) = 0` (when `Z ŌēĀ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_╬▓(c ŌĆó A) = c┬▓ ┬Ę Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(c ŌĆó 1) = 0` (when `Z ŌēĀ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_╬▓(ŌłÆA) = Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(A + c ŌĆó 1) = Var_╬▓(A)` (when `Z ŌēĀ 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsExpectation_star_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Ō¤©A ┬Ę BŌ¤®_╬▓ = Ō¤©B ┬Ę AŌ¤®_╬▓` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ┬¦3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬¦3.5, p. 89.

| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `╬ø ŌåÆ ╬ø ŌåÆ Ōäé` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |

| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) ŌåÆ Fin (N+2) ŌåÆ Ōäé`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

(4.3.4)) ŌĆö the provable finite-volume groundwork for the thermodynamic-limit bridge (Issue #4564). Axiom-free | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |

(4.3.4)): for `0 < d`, `0 < N` the finite-volume per-bond ground-state energy density converges as `nŌåÆŌł×` to the infinite-volume energy density `╬Ą_GS(d,N)` (existentially pinned to the genuine limit ŌĆö the thermodynamic-limit existence is the deep analytic content) | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |

(4.3.4)); the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omega0` (symmetric infinite-volume ground state `Žē_0` exists for the box AFM model's limit, via Theorem 4.20, eqs. (4.3.7)/(4.3.9)); and the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omegaN` (the symmetry-breaking ground states `Žē_n` with N├®el magnetization `(ŌłÆ1)^x mŌłŚ n_╬▒`, given `HasStaggeredLRO`, eqs. | `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(Ō¤©H_periodicŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(Ō¤©H_periodic ┬Ę OŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(Ō¤©H_periodic^2Ō¤®_╬▓).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(Ō¤©H_periodic^nŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` for any `n : ŌäĢ` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Ō¤©A┬ĘB + B┬ĘAŌ¤®_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Ō¤©A┬ĘB ŌłÆ B┬ĘAŌ¤®_╬▓).re = 0` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_╬▓(H_periodic)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenberg_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ╬▓ H_periodic).im = 0` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓).re : Ōäé) = Ō¤©OŌ¤®_╬▓` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(Žü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for the periodic-chain Hamiltonian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J ┬Ę spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki ┬¦2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ę |ŌåæŌåæŌ¤® = -(J/2) ┬Ę |ŌåæŌåæŌ¤®` ŌĆö `|ŌåæŌåæŌ¤®` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ę (|ŌåæŌåōŌ¤® - |ŌåōŌåæŌ¤®) = (3J/2) ┬Ę (|ŌåæŌåōŌ¤® - |ŌåōŌåæŌ¤®)` ŌĆö singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ę |ŌåōŌåōŌ¤® = -(J/2) ┬Ę |ŌåōŌåōŌ¤®` ŌĆö all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ┬Ę (|ŌåæŌåōŌ¤® + |ŌåōŌåæŌ¤®) = -(J/2) ┬Ę (|ŌåæŌåōŌ¤® + |ŌåōŌåæŌ¤®)` ŌĆö triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ┬Ę (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` ŌĆö explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ┬Ę |ŌåæŌåæŌåæŌ¤® = -J ┬Ę |ŌåæŌåæŌåæŌ¤®` ŌĆö confirming the linear scaling `E(|Ōåæ..ŌåæŌ¤®) = -N┬ĘJ/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : ŌäĢ`, `╬Ż_{x,y Ōłł Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ┬Ę J : Ōäé)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : ŌäĢ`, `J : ŌäØ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ┬Ę |s..sŌ¤® = -(N┬ĘJ/2 : Ōäé) ┬Ę |s..sŌ¤®` ŌĆö both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ┬¦2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|┬ĘS┬▓` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N┬ĘJ/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : ŌäĢ`, `J : ŌäØ`, `k : ŌäĢ`, `H_open ┬Ę ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬Ę |Ōåæ..ŌåæŌ¤®) = -(N┬ĘJ/2 : Ōäé) ┬Ę ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬Ę |Ōåæ..ŌåæŌ¤®)` ŌĆö the unnormalised Tasaki ┬¦2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|╬”_MŌ¤®` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|Ōåō..ŌåōŌ¤®`, same eigenvalue `-(N┬ĘJ/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AŌĆōC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal ŌåÆ primitive (Seneta ┬¦1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ┬¦1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) Ōēż (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)┬Ęx Ōēż Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ┬¦1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r┬Ęv`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` Ō¤╣ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ╬╝v`, `v Ōēź 0`, `v ŌēĀ 0` Ō¤╣ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg Ō¤╣ Ōłā `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬¦1.2, pp. 27ŌĆō28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg Ō¤╣ Ōłā `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬¦1.2, pp. 27ŌĆō28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian Ō¤╣ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `╬╝` Ō¤╣ every real eigenvector at `╬╝` is `s ŌĆó v` for some scalar `s` (the `╬╝`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t ŌĆó u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `╬╝`, hence `ŌłØ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u ŌłØ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬¦1.2 (pp. 27ŌĆō28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallŌĆōLiebŌĆōMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> ŌĆö the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(┼£Ōü╗_tot)^M|all-upŌ¤®` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0ŌåÆ1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronŌĆōFrobenius ground state
> (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[─ż, ┼£Ōü╗_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { Žā : V ŌåÆ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS Žā = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). The two bundled sector-level MLM Theorem 2.2
ground-state results (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_groundState`,
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`) were
subsequently relocated to
`Quantum/SpinS/MarshallLiebMattisSectorBundled.lean` (#4569, re-proved via the
canonical `*Structural*` twins). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ╬”` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `╬╝` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `╬│` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card┬ĘN/2ŌłÆM)(card┬ĘN/2ŌłÆMŌłÆ1)`, then `┼£Ōü╗_tot ┬Ę magSectorEmbedding ╬”` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `╬╝` (via `[─ż, ┼£Ōü╗_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronŌĆōFrobenius ground states (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `┼£Ōü║_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card┬ĘN/2ŌłÆK)(card┬ĘN/2ŌłÆK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `┼£_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A Žā = Ōłæ_{xŌłłA}(N/2ŌłÆŽā_x)`. Disjointness of distinct eigenvalues and basis-state membership (Submodule membership uses mathlib's standard `.zero_mem`/`.add_mem`/`.smul_mem`/ŌĆ” directly ŌĆö no dedicated wrapper lemmas). Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(┼£_A)┬▓`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(┼£_A)┬▓`/`(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` eigenvalues `╬│_A, ╬│_B` obey `╬│_A.re Ōēż s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B.re Ōēż s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralPFJointCasimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬ĘN`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|┬ĘN/2 ŌłÆ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A ŌłÆ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬ĘN`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `╬╝ Ōēż predicted ŌłÆ s_A(s_A+1) ŌłÆ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness ŌĆö exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `╬╝`) has `╬╝ Ōēż E` for every sector eigenvector `Žå ŌēĀ 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted ŌłÆ s_A(s_A+1) ŌłÆ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_AŌłÆs_B|`, a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `╬│_A Ōēż s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B Ōēż s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(╬│_totŌłÆ╬│_AŌłÆ╬│_B).re Ōēż predicted ŌłÆ s_A(s_A+1) ŌłÆ s_B(s_B+1)` has `╬│_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschŌĆōGordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(┼£_tot)┬▓ Ōēź m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 Ōēż M.re` has `M.re┬Ę(M.re+1) Ōēż ╬│.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `┼£Ōü║_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' Ōēź M`, giving `╬│ = M'(M'+1) Ōēź M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_AŌłÆs_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschŌĆōGordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼£_tot)┬▓`, `(┼£_A)┬▓`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` at `(╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B)` is a `─ż_toy`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot ŌłÆ ╬│_A ŌłÆ ╬│_B` (from `─ż_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ŌłÆ(┼£_A)┬▓ŌłÆ(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_le_sA` | **Sublattice Casimir spectral max bound** `(┼£_A)┬▓ Ōēż s_A(s_A+1)`: every eigenvalue `╬│` of `(┼£_A)┬▓` (with a non-zero eigenvector) has `╬│.re Ōēż s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬ĘN/2`. Assembles the sublattice weight-component extraction (#3670), highest-weight existence (#3671), and the highest-weight bound (#3668). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; the sublattice analytic ingredient of the Issue #3658 witness construction (Issue #3658, PR 12). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirSpectralBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeExists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_ne_sMax_add_one`) | **Sublattice highest-weight existence**: from a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_AŌłÆk)`, repeatedly raising with `┼£_A^+` (which terminates once the sublattice magnetization exceeds `s_A`, since the subspace at `s_A+1` is `ŌŖź`) produces a non-zero highest-weight `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at the same `╬│`. Sublattice analogue of `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (Issue #3658, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeightExistence.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (with `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice weight-component extraction**: since `(┼£_A)┬▓` commutes with `┼£_A^(3)` it preserves each sublattice magnetization subspace and commutes with the projector; hence a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` has a non-zero sublattice-weight component `sublatticeMagProjFn A (s_AŌłÆk) v` that is again a `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in a single sublattice magnetization subspace. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (Issue #3658, PR 10). Apply the anticommutation `╬ś ┼£┬│_tot = -┼£┬│_tot ╬ś` (#3744) to ╬”: get `╬┤╬│ ŌĆó ╬” = -╬│╬┤ ŌĆó ╬”` Ō¤╣ `2╬┤╬│ ŌĆó ╬” = 0` Ō¤╣ `╬┤╬│ = 0` Ō¤╣ `╬│ = 0` (since ╬┤ ŌēĀ 0). | `complex_dressed_parity_block_submatrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` | **(f.3-finish) complex dressed parity-block submatrix `finrank Ōäé Ōēż 1` at the PF eigenvalue** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3831): combines the parity-block real-form PerronŌĆōFrobenius simplicity (`...ReMatrixOnParityBlock_eigenspace_finrank_le_one`, #3827) with the real-to-complex eigenspace bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` (#3828) via the matrix identity `(dressed_re.submatrix valS valS).map ((Ōåæ) : ŌäØ ŌåÆ Ōäé) = dressed_complex.submatrix valS valS` ŌĆö which holds because every entry of `dressed_complex` is real under real coefficients (#3830 `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_apply_im_zero` lifting #3829 `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_apply_im_zero` through the Marshall sign factors). Statement: under physical hyps on `J, lam, D`, `Ōłā ╬Į : ŌäØ, finrank Ōäé (eig dressed_─ż'.submatrix valS valS) (╬Į : Ōäé) Ōēż 1` for the parity-`p` submatrix. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ŌĆō44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ComplexDressedParityBlockFinrank.lean`) |
| `parity_block_full_eigenspace_inter_finrank_le_submatrix` (with `parityRestrictMap`, `ker_magParityDiagS_apply_eq_zero_of_parity_ne`, `sum_parityConfigS_eq_sum_filter`) | **(f.4) block-diag bridge** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3832): for a parity-block-diagonal complex matrix `M`, the restriction-to-parity-`p` map `parityRestrictMap : (eig M ╬Į ŌŖō ker(P=(-1)^p)) ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] eig (M.submatrix valS valS) ╬Į` is injective (image lands in the submatrix eigenspace via the block-diag identity; injectivity uses that vectors in `ker(P=(-1)^p)` vanish off parityConfigS p). Hence `finrank Ōäé (eig M ╬Į ŌŖō ker(P=(-1)^p)) Ōēż finrank Ōäé (eig M.submatrix ╬Į)` for `p < 2`. The bridge for lifting the per-block PF bound (#3831) to the full eigenspace ŌŖō parity ker. | `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one` (with `..._apply_eq_zero_of_magSum_parity_ne`, `..._commute_magParityDiagS`, `..._eigenspace_finrank_le_parity_blocks`) | **(g.1) dressed `─ż'` eigenspace `finrank Ōäé Ōēż 2` via parity-block decomposition** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3833): the dressed axis-swapped Hamiltonian (i) inherits parity-block-diagonality from the bare via `dressed Žā Žä = m_A(Žā) m_A(Žä) * bare Žā Žä`, (ii) commutes with `P = magParityDiagS` entrywise (since `╬ś_A` and `P` are both diagonal, hence the entire `╬ś_A ─ż' ╬ś_A` commutes with `P`), (iii) so `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) applies and the eigenspace splits across `┬▒1` parity blocks. Given per-block `Ōēż 1` bounds, full dressed eig `Ōēż 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ŌĆō44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/DressedAxisSwapDegeneracyBound.lean`) |
| `dressed_full_eigenspace_inter_parity_finrank_le_one` | **(g.2) dressed `─ż'` per-block full-eigenspace `finrank Ōäé Ōēż 1` at the PF ╬Į** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3834): combines #3831 (parity-submatrix eig Ōēż 1) with #3832 (block-diag bridge) under the dressed parity-block-diagonality (#3833 step 1). Result: `Ōłā ╬Į : ŌäØ, finrank Ōäé (eig dressed_─ż' ╬Į ŌŖō ker(P=(-1)^p)) Ōēż 1` for `p < 2`. The per-block input consumed by the dressed eig Ōēż 2 assembly. | `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank Ōäé (eig H' ╬╝ ŌŖō ker(Q=c)) = finrank Ōäé (eig H ╬╝ ŌŖō ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `─ż'_dressed` to the bare `─ż'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `╬ś_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ŌĆō44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/GaugeIntersectionEigenspaceFinrank.lean`) |
| `bare_axisSwapped_full_eigenspace_inter_parity_finrank_le_one` (with `marshallDiagonal_commute_magParityDiagS`) | **(g.4) bare `─ż'` per-block PF `finrank Ōäé Ōēż 1` via Marshall similarity** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3836): transfers the dressed `─ż'_dressed` per-block bound (#3834) to the bare axis-swapped `─ż'` via the Marshall similarity for intersected eigenspaces (#3835). Key observation: the Marshall sign diagonal `╬ś_A` and the magnetization parity diagonal `P = magParityDiagS` are both diagonal and hence commute, so the intersection `eig ─ż' ╬╝ ŌŖō ker(P=c)` is preserved by the Marshall similarity dressed ŌåÆ bare. Result for `p < 2`: `Ōłā ╬Į : ŌäØ, finrank Ōäé (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (╬Į : Ōäé) ŌŖō ker(P=(-1)^p)) Ōēż 1`. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one` (with `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`) | **(g.5) bare anisotropic `─ż` eigenspace `finrank Ōäé Ōēż 2` (conditional, via axis-swap)** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3837): wraps the conditional `bare ─ż' eig Ōēż 2 from per-block Ōēż 1` (`axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`, #3776 family) with the axis-swap unitary equivalence (`AxisSwapUnitaryS.anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, #3753) to give the same conditional bound for the bare anisotropic Hamiltonian `─ż`. General `N` version requires an explicit `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. This **closes the bare `─ż` Ōēż 2 link in the (g) chain**, conditional on per-parity-block PF hypotheses (discharged separately per sector by #3836). The remaining gap to unconditional obligation (1) is the ╬Į-sector matching (PF eigenvalues of even/odd sectors coinciding at the ground-state energy), which is mathematically distinct (Hermitian spectral minimisation) and deferred. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ŌĆō44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicEigLeTwoConditional.lean`) |
| `parity_block_submatrix_full_inter_finrank_eq` (with `parity_block_submatrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_full_inter`, `parityEmbedMap`) | **(h.1) reverse block-diag bridge** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3840): for a parity-block-diagonal complex `M`, the parity-`p` submatrix's ╬Į-eigenspace embeds INJECTIVELY into `eig M ╬Į ŌŖō ker(P=(-1)^p)` via the zero-extension map (extend by 0 off `parityConfigS p`). Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank Ōäé (eig M.submatrix ╬Į) = finrank Ōäé (eig M ╬Į ŌŖō ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks ╬Į-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig ŌŖō ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ŌĆō44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockDiagSubmatrixEmbed.lean`) |
| `eigenspace_finrank_eq_of_commuting_involution` | **(h.2) involution eigenspace decomposition ŌĆö finrank EQUALITY** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3841): strengthens `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) from `Ōēż` to `=`. For finite-dim `T, P` with `TŌłśP = PŌłśT` and `PŌłśP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `╬╝` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `┬▒1`. The `Ōēż` was tight because the two intersected `┬▒1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). Used by (h.3) to derive the block-sum equality. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ŌĆō44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/InvolutionEigenspaceDecompEq.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_eq_sum_parity_blocks` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`) | **(h.3) block-sum finrank equality** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3842): combines (h.1) #3840 and (h.2) #3841 to give the block-sum equality for any parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `magParityDiagS`: `finrank Ōäé (eig M ╬╝) = finrank Ōäé (eig M.submatrix_0 ╬╝) + finrank Ōäé (eig M.submatrix_1 ╬╝)`. Specialised to the dressed `─ż'_dressed` and the bare `─ż'` (both block-diagonal and commuting with `P`). Central tool for unconditional `Ōēż 2` at any ╬╝ where both per-block bounds hold simultaneously. Combined with PF (per-sector simplicity at the per-sector spectrum minimum) and a Hermitian spectral identification of the GS energy as `min(╬Į_0, ╬Į_1)`, gives the unconditional bare `─ż'` GS degeneracy `Ōēż 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ŌĆō44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockSumFinrankEq.lean`) |

The PR exposes no-argument wrappers for the axis-swap eigenspace-dimension equality, the conditional parity-block and submatrix-block `Ōēż 2` bounds, the min-block `Ōēż 2` bound, the Hermitian-minimum equality, and the structural general-N capstone `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general`. This discharges the explicit axis-swap-unitary argument in the strict case-(i) degeneracy chain; the SU(2)-uniqueness target wrappers are handled in the later obligation-(2) row, and Tasaki case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges recorded in the case-(ii) rows, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_sector_pf_at_target` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S target conditional bridge with balanced-sector PF discharged** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): packages the final linear-algebra step after the general spin-S `finrank Ōēż 2` degeneracy capstone. If the target full ground eigenspace has `finrank Ōēż 2`, the balanced sector attains the full ground energy, and the balanced-sector ground eigenspace has `finrank Ōēż 1`, then the full target ground eigenspace has `finrank Ōēż 1`; the companion wrapper then gives zero total `┼£┬│` magnetization for every non-zero target ground state. The new general spin-S PF wrapper chooses a finite strict diagonal shift and applies the existing anisotropic sector PF/minimum theorem, so the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit balanced-sector PF callback. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43ŌĆō44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetConditionalBridge.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSBalancedSectorPFTarget.lean`) |

These first case-(ii) wrappers keep the remaining target inputs explicit as balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2`; they do not reuse the case-(i) axis-swap sign argument. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input from the first case-(ii) wrappers is replaced by the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` over every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector under `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`; the remaining full ground eigenspace `finrank <= 2` input stays explicit in this intermediate wrapper. This is a case-(ii) bridge step, not the final case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_groundState_mem_balanced_sector_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` | **General spin-S target bridge from strict sector gap without the full `finrank <= 2` input** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that if the balanced sector attains the full ground energy and every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector has strictly larger minimum, then every full ground vector lies in the balanced magnetization sector. This uses the sector-projection eigenvector bridge and `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min`, not the case-(i) parity-block `finrank <= 2` contradiction. Composing this containment with balanced-sector Perron--Frobenius simplicity gives full target uniqueness and zero total magnetization from strict sector gap alone; the case-(ii)-named wrappers keep only the theorem-facing `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` hypotheses plus the strict-gap input. This removes the explicit case-(ii) full `finrank <= 2` input; deriving the case-(ii) strict gap remains future work. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetFromStrictGapNoFullFinrank.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_strict_gap_all_M_of_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_crossing_contradiction` | **General spin-S case-(ii) strict-gap and target bridge from a crossing contradiction callback** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): compresses the remaining case-(ii) strict-gap derivation to one theorem-shaped callback. If every non-balanced target crossing `E_M <= E_balanced` contradicts the case-(ii) hypotheses `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`, then order duality gives the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` for every non-balanced non-empty sector. The target wrappers feed that strict gap to the no-full-finrank bridge above, so the public endpoint surface no longer exposes either balanced/full equality, full `finrank <= 2`, or strict gap directly; the remaining mathematical task is proving the crossing contradiction callback. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapFromCrossing.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_crossing_contradiction_of_path_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_path_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_path_crossing_contradiction` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from a path crossing contradiction callback** (Tasaki ┬¦2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): evaluates the case-(ii) deformation path at `t = 1`. If every crossing along the path inside the region `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` contradicts the path hypotheses, then a target crossing `E_M(lambda,D) <= E_balanced(lambda,D)` is impossible. The target wrappers feed this contradiction to the crossing-callback bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the path crossing contradiction callback itself. Therefore, if every non-balanced non-empty sector has contradictory crossing-set non-emptiness, every path crossing is impossible, and the target wrappers feed that path contradiction to the path-callback bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the crossing-set non-emptiness contradiction callback, matching the first-crossing `hne` surface used by the existing scaffold. Thus a contradiction at that first-crossing point rules out crossing-set non-emptiness, and the target wrappers feed the result to the crossing-set bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the first-crossing contradiction callback itself.
